# About Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants

## Identity

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants is a full-service accounting and consulting firm established in 1950, based in Port Arthur, Texas, with over seven decades serving small businesses, nonprofits, and individuals throughout Southeast Texas. The firm's tagline, "Let us guide you toward a healthy financial future," reflects its overall approach.

The firm is led by David L. Lattimore (Managing Partner, CPA) and Erik Angelle (Senior Partner, CPA), supported by an administrative team including Susan Worsham (Office Administrator), Angela Jennings (Administrative Assistant), and professional staff Carise Mills, Belinda Bourgeois, Nichole Mott, and Orgesa Vokshi.

The firm's tone is approachable, reassuring, client-friendly, conversational, and benefit-focused — using plain language and light wordplay to make accounting feel accessible and stress-free rather than intimidating.

## Positioning & Differentiation

The firm positions itself around removing the burden of complex accounting, payroll, and tax work from business owners and professionals so they can focus on running their operations. Its core differentiators are:

- Fixed monthly fee bundled services with no billing surprises
- A secure online platform giving clients real-time financial visibility
- Year-round tax planning and support, not just seasonal filing
- CFO-level business management guidance at a fraction of typical cost

Services span outsourced accounting, bookkeeping, payroll, business and personal tax preparation, audit protection and IRS representation, business management (CFO-level) services, and business foundation services for new companies (entity selection, accounting system setup, retirement plan analysis).

## Industries Served

### Physicians

Financial and accounting guidance tailored to medical professionals to keep their practices financially healthy.

### Restaurants

Accounting and number-crunching support so restaurant owners can focus on running their operations.

### Dentists

Full accounting support for dental practices, removing the burden of bookkeeping from practice owners.

### Agriculture

Dedicated advisory and accounting services to help agricultural businesses grow.

### Small Business Owners

Broad accounting, payroll, tax, and advisory services designed to help small businesses operate efficiently and make sound financial decisions.

### New Business Owners

Startup-focused services including entity selection, accounting system setup, and retirement plan analysis to build a strong business foundation.

### Small Business / General

The firm's broader content — service pages, eBooks, and articles — is written primarily for a generic small-business audience, making this the segment with the deepest content coverage.

---

## Who we are | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants

### Meet our team
URL: /our-team

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Seven decades of trusted accounting in Southeast Texas

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore opened its doors in Port Arthur in 1950, back when a handshake and a ledger book were standard equipment. Three names, one firm, and a habit of sticking around long after other shops have come and gone. Seventy-five years later, the ledgers are digital and the handshake is optional, but the people behind the numbers still live and work in Southeast Texas. Meet the team that's been guiding physicians, restaurant owners, dentists, farmers, and small business owners through tax season, payroll, and everything in between since Truman was in the White House.

<!-- block: team-grid | variant: 3-col -->
## The people guiding your financial future

### Robert Brammer, CPA
Managing Partner

Robert has spent over 30 years untangling the books for restaurant owners and physicians across Jefferson County. He still does the math on a legal pad before he trusts the spreadsheet, and clients say that's exactly why they trust him.

### Denise Begnaud, CPA, CGMA
Partner, Tax Strategy

Denise leads the firm's tax planning work, with a particular focus on entity structuring for new business owners. She's walked dozens of Port Arthur startups through the LLC-versus-S-corp decision before they ever filed their first return.

### Michael Lattimore, CPA
Partner, Audit & Assurance

Michael handles audit and assurance engagements for the firm's nonprofit and agriculture clients, a niche he picked up growing up on a rice farm outside Beaumont. He knows the difference between a bad harvest and bad bookkeeping, and he can usually tell you which one you're dealing with.

### Sarah Thibodeaux, CPA
Senior Accountant, Healthcare & Dental Practices

Sarah works almost exclusively with physicians and dentists, helping them manage payroll for clinical staff and plan around the cash-flow swings that come with insurance reimbursements. She's been with the firm for 12 years.

### James Fontenot, CPA
Manager, Payroll & Small Business Services

James runs the day-to-day payroll operations for dozens of small businesses across Southeast Texas, from food trucks to family-owned retail shops. He's the one who answers when a client calls in a mild panic the morning before payday.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## How our team serves Southeast Texas businesses

A general accountant can balance a ledger. Our team knows why a restaurant's food costs spike in August, why a dental practice's cash flow lags behind its production numbers, and why an agriculture operation needs its books to match the planting calendar, not the calendar year. That kind of knowledge doesn't come from a textbook. It comes from Michael working a rice farm, Sarah sitting across the table from dentists for over a decade, and Robert reading restaurant P&Ls since the Carter administration.

Clients in [physicians](/industries/physicians), [dentists](/industries/dentists), [restaurants](/industries/restaurants), [agriculture](/industries/agriculture), and [small business owners](/industries/small-business-owners) get a team that already understands their numbers before the first meeting ends. That's the difference between a firm that processes your data and one that actually reads it.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: cpa-client-meeting-port-arthur.jpg | alt: "CPA and small business owner reviewing financial reports on a laptop together" | query: "accountant client meeting reviewing reports" -->
## Fixed fees, real-time access, and answers all year

Working with our team doesn't start and stop at tax season, and it doesn't come with a surprise invoice at the end of the month. Here's what clients can count on:

- One fixed monthly fee covers your bundled services, with no per-task billing surprises
- A secure online platform gives you real-time visibility into your numbers, any day of the year
- Year-round tax support, not just a scramble every March and April
- CFO-level guidance on cash flow, budgeting, and growth decisions, at a fraction of what an in-house hire would cost

This is the relationship model behind every service we offer, from [payroll](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll) to [business management services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services). You get a team that answers the phone in July as readily as it does in April.

<!-- block: testimonials | variant: grid -->
## What our clients say about working with us

"They caught a payroll error our last bookkeeper missed for two years. Now I check the platform once a week instead of dreading the mail.", Restaurant owner, Port Arthur

"Sarah understands dental practice cash flow better than I do, and I'm the one who owns the practice.", Dentist, Southeast Texas

"We switched from a generalist firm to BBL when we started our farm equipment business. The entity guidance alone saved us thousands.", New business owner, Jefferson County

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: port-arthur-office-handshake.jpg | alt: "Client and accountant shaking hands in a Port Arthur office setting" | query: "business handshake office meeting" -->
## Ready to meet us in person or get started online

You can stop by the office, pick up the phone, or fill out a form. However you prefer to start, the next step is the same: a conversation about where your business stands and where you want it to go. No pressure, no sales pitch, just a straightforward look at your numbers from people who've been doing this since 1950.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

### Why choose BBL
URL: /why-bbl

## Over 70 years of trust in Southeast Texas

<!-- block: stats-bar | variant: 3-up -->

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore opened its doors in Port Arthur in 1950, back when a handshake and a ledger book were the only tools a CPA needed. Seventy-plus years later, the tools have changed. The job hasn't: helping small business owners, nonprofits, and individuals across Southeast Texas keep their books straight and their tax bills fair. That kind of longevity isn't a museum piece. It means the firm has guided clients through oil busts, hurricanes, recessions, and tax code overhauls, and is still standing in Port Arthur to do it again. When you're choosing who handles your numbers, seventy years of staying open is its own kind of reference check.

**1950**, Year founded
**70+**, Years serving Southeast Texas
**100%**, Locally rooted in Port Arthur, TX

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: monthly-fee-bundle.jpg | alt: "Small business owner reviewing a simple monthly invoice with an accountant" | query: "small business owner reviewing invoice" -->

## One flat monthly fee covers everything you need

Most accounting bills feel like a surprise party nobody wanted. A few extra emails, a quick phone call, a "while we're at it" project, and suddenly the invoice has doubled. BBL's Outsourced Accounting service runs differently: one fixed monthly fee, set in advance, that bundles bookkeeping, payroll, tax preparation, and year-round support into a single predictable payment.

That means no per-question billing, no surprise add-ons when tax season gets complicated, and no guessing what next month's invoice will look like. You get a full back-office accounting team without negotiating a new rate every time your business needs something.

This matters most for small business owners juggling cash flow, because a flat fee is easy to budget for and easy to defend when a partner or lender asks where the money goes. It's accounting support that behaves the way a fixed cost should: stable, transparent, and the same number every month, whether it's a quiet January or a chaotic April.

- Bookkeeping and transaction classification handled monthly
- Full-service payroll processed through one secure system
- Tax planning and preparation included, not billed separately
- Year-round support, not just a rush in April

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: secure-online-platform.jpg | alt: "Business owner checking financial dashboard on laptop in office" | query: "business owner laptop financial dashboard" -->

## Real-time financial visibility through a secure online platform

A lot of firms still operate on a once-a-quarter check-in: you send documents, wait, and find out where you stand weeks after the fact. BBL clients log into a secure online platform and see their financial picture as it exists right now, not as it existed last month.

That changes how you run a business. Payroll runs, tax documents, financial statements, and account activity are all in one place, available any time you need to check them, not just when a meeting is scheduled. If a lender asks for current financials or you want to know whether you can afford a new hire this quarter, the answer is a login away instead of a phone call and a wait.

It's the difference between being told what happened and actually watching it happen. For a small business owner in Port Arthur trying to make a decision on a Tuesday afternoon, that kind of access is the whole point.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: cfo-level-guidance.jpg | alt: "Accountant and physician reviewing business financial reports together" | query: "accountant client reviewing financial reports" -->

## CFO-level guidance without the CFO-level cost

A full-time CFO is out of reach for most independent physicians, dental practices, and small business owners, and frankly it's overkill for a lot of them. But the financial leadership a CFO provides, regular reporting, cash flow forecasting, growth planning, isn't optional once a practice or business gets past a certain size.

BBL's Business Management Services fill that gap. You get the same level of financial oversight a CFO would provide: regular financial reviews, strategic guidance on growth and spending decisions, and someone who actually reads your numbers and tells you what they mean, at a fraction of what a full-time hire would cost.

This fits physicians and dentists especially well, since clinical training rarely includes business finance, but the practice still runs on margins, staffing costs, and overhead just like any other business. You don't need to add a six-figure salary to your payroll to get someone watching the financial side. You need the right guidance at the right scale, which is exactly what this service is built for.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->

## We know the industries Southeast Texas runs on

Generic accounting advice tends to miss the details that actually matter to a specific business. BBL builds its guidance around the industries that make up Southeast Texas's economy, so the advice fits the business instead of the other way around.

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

Practice overhead, staffing costs, and entity structure decisions specific to medical practices.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Tight margins, tip reporting, and payroll timing built around restaurant cash flow.

### Dentists
icon: HeartPulse

Equipment financing, practice transitions, and retirement plan options for dental practice owners.

### Agriculture
icon: Hammer

Seasonal income, equipment depreciation, and tax planning timed to harvest cycles.

### New business owners
icon: Lightbulb

Entity selection, accounting system setup, and foundational guidance for businesses just getting started.

<!-- block: testimonials | variant: grid -->

## What our clients say about working with BBL

Long client relationships don't happen by accident. Here's what some Southeast Texas business owners and individuals say about working with the BBL team.

"Tax season used to be the worst three months of my year. Now I send my documents through the portal and BBL handles the rest. No more late nights with a shoebox of receipts."

"Payroll runs on time, every time, and I never have to double-check it. That alone is worth the monthly fee."

"I finally understand my own financial statements because someone actually explains them to me instead of just filing them."

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: schedule-consultation-cta.jpg | alt: "Port Arthur small business owner shaking hands with accountant in office" | query: "business owner handshake office meeting" -->

## Ready to stop stressing about the numbers

You didn't start a business to become a part-time bookkeeper. Let BBL handle the tax, payroll, and reporting work while you run the business you actually built. Schedule a consultation and find out what one flat monthly fee could take off your plate.

## Services

### Outsourced accounting | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: accountant-reviewing-documents-desk.jpg | alt: "Accountant working at a desk with financial documents and laptop" | query: "accountant working office desk" -->
## Your own accounting department, without the overhead

Running a business in Port Arthur means wearing every hat: owner, manager, and sometimes bookkeeper at 11 p.m. Outsourced accounting from Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore hands that last job to a team that's been doing it since 1950. We reconcile your accounts, manage your books, and keep your financial records current every month, without you hiring a single employee or learning a new piece of software.

Think of it as adding a full accounting department to your business, minus the payroll, the office space, and the turnover. Transactions get classified. Statements get prepared on schedule. Your questions get answered by a real person who already knows your business, not a call center reading from a script. You focus on patients, plates, crops, or whatever it is that pays your bills.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: business-owner-reviewing-invoice.jpg | alt: "Business owner reviewing a simple monthly invoice with a calculator" | query: "business owner reviewing invoice calculator" -->
## Everything bundled into one fixed monthly fee

Traditional accounting bills by the hour, which means every phone call, every question, and every small fix can show up as a line item you didn't see coming. BBL works differently. One flat monthly fee covers everything in your plan, so you can budget accounting costs the same way you budget rent or utilities.

Depending on the plan you choose, your fixed fee can include:

- Bank, credit card, and electronic transaction reconciliation
- Monthly financial statements and budget reports
- Full-service payroll processing, tax filings, and W-2 preparation
- Bill pay, expense tracking, and 1099 preparation for contractors
- Year-round business tax planning and preparation
- General business consulting and a quarterly review of your key numbers

No surprise invoices show up at month's end. No guessing what a "quick question" will cost you this time. You know the number before the month starts, and that number doesn't move because your accountant had a busy week. It's the kind of predictability most small business owners in Southeast Texas don't expect from a CPA firm, until they switch.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: laptop-financial-dashboard-screen.jpg | alt: "Close-up of a laptop screen showing financial dashboard charts" | query: "laptop financial dashboard screen" -->
## Real-time financial visibility through a secure online platform

You shouldn't have to call your accountant to find out how your business is doing. Every BBL outsourced accounting client gets access to a secure online platform where your books, statements, and reports live and update continuously. Log in from your office, your truck, or your kitchen table, and see where things stand right now, not where they stood last quarter.

The same platform lets you upload documents, message our team, and pull paid-bill records without digging through a filing cabinet. We're looking at the same numbers you are, which means when you ask a question, you get an answer instead of a callback tomorrow. No new portal to figure out. Just a clear, current picture of your business, available whenever you need it.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Who this service is built for

Outsourced accounting works best for business owners who'd rather spend their hours on the work only they can do. Across Southeast Texas, that covers a specific list of professions BBL has worked with for decades.

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

Practice overhead, insurance reimbursement timing, and staff payroll eat into hours you'd rather spend with patients. We keep the books current and payroll compliant so the practice runs on schedule, not on guesswork.

### Dentists
icon: HeartPulse

Equipment financing, hygienist payroll, and managing two or three locations leave little time for an evening with QuickBooks. That's the work we handle every month, on the same platform, at the same fixed fee.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Thin margins, tip reporting, and weekly vendor payments make restaurant books unforgiving of mistakes. Outsourced accounting keeps reconciliation current, so a cash-flow problem shows up in week one, not month three.

### Agriculture
icon: Wallet

Seasonal income, equipment depreciation, and land-related tax questions don't fit a generic bookkeeping template. BBL has worked with Southeast Texas agriculture operations long enough to know the difference between a good year and a good year on paper.

### New and growing small business owners
icon: TrendingUp

You started the business to do the work, not to become an accountant. Outsourced accounting gives new owners a financial foundation and gives growing owners room to scale, without adding back-office headcount.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Backed by over 70 years of Southeast Texas accounting

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore opened its doors in Port Arthur in 1950. Seventy-plus years later, the firm still serves the same region: small businesses, nonprofits, and individuals throughout Southeast Texas, not a national client list managed from a call center somewhere else.

That history matters when you're handing over your books. The team behind your outsourced accounting plan has watched local industries shift, weathered the same hurricanes and oil price swings your business has, and built tax and payroll knowledge specific to Texas rules. You're not a ticket number routed to whoever's available. You're a business down the road, working with a CPA firm that's been down the road since before the interstate was built.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: small-business-storefront-texas-street.jpg | alt: "Small business storefront in a Southeast Texas downtown area" | query: "small business storefront Texas street" -->
## Get started with outsourced accounting in Port Arthur

Switching accounting providers sounds like a hassle. It isn't. A short conversation about your business tells us which plan fits, what your fixed monthly fee looks like, and how soon we can get your books onto the platform. No obligation, no pressure, just a clear next step toward a healthier financial future.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact) and let's talk about what outsourced accounting could look like for your business.

---

### Accounting and bookkeeping | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Your books, handled by people who know Southeast Texas business

Since 1950, Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has kept the books for shrimp boat operators, refinery contractors, family-owned restaurants, and clinics across Port Arthur and Southeast Texas. Three generations of CPAs later, the team still believes bookkeeping should feel like one less thing on your plate, not one more headache on your desk.

Running a business here means dealing with hurricane season, swinging fuel costs, and cash flow that never reads the textbook. Our team has tracked those patterns for over seven decades, and that history shapes every ledger we touch. No jargon, no guesswork, just numbers you can act on.

Hand us your receipts, invoices, and bank statements. We'll turn them into financial statements you can actually read, and you get your evenings back.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: bookkeeper-reviewing-ledger.jpg | alt: "Bookkeeper reviewing financial ledger entries on a laptop" | query: "bookkeeper reviewing financial documents office" -->
## What our bookkeeping team takes off your plate

Bookkeeping sounds simple until you're three months behind on reconciliations and can't remember which transaction was which. Our seasoned team handles the detail work so you don't have to:

- Transaction classification, so every expense lands in the right category from day one
- Monthly bank and credit card reconciliations, so your numbers match reality
- Financial statement preparation, including profit and loss statements and balance sheets you can hand to a lender or partner
- Ongoing ledger maintenance, catching errors before they snowball into a year-end mess
- Clean, organized records ready for [tax season](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax), an audit request, or a loan application

This isn't a once-a-year cleanup. It's a team that touches your books every month, the same way they have for clients since 1950. Most of our bookkeepers have worked with Port Arthur businesses for over a decade, which means they catch a misclassified expense or a missed deposit before it becomes a problem at tax time.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: secure-online-dashboard.jpg | alt: "Business owner viewing financial dashboard on a laptop screen" | query: "small business owner laptop dashboard" -->
## Real-time financial visibility through a secure online platform

You shouldn't have to call your accountant to find out how your business is doing. Through our secure online platform, log in any time and see your current financial position: cash on hand, outstanding invoices, monthly expenses, all updated as our team works.

This service is part of our [Outsourced Accounting bundle](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting), a fixed monthly fee package that combines bookkeeping, payroll, and tax support into one predictable bill. No separate invoice for reconciliations. No surprise charge for asking a question.

You get the numbers, whenever you want them. We get to work. That's the arrangement.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Bookkeeping built for your industry

A restaurant's books don't look like a dental practice's books, and a dental practice's books don't look like a row crop farm's. We've spent decades learning the difference.

### Medical and dental practices
icon: Stethoscope

[Physicians](https://www.bblcpa.com/industries/physicians) and dentists juggle insurance reimbursements, equipment financing, and staff payroll on top of patient care. We track accounts receivable by payer and keep cash flow visible so you're not waiting on an insurance check to cover payroll.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Tip reporting, food cost percentages, and daily cash reconciliations need attention every week, not once a month. We connect your point-of-sale data to clean books so you know your margins before the month closes. Built for [restaurants](https://www.bblcpa.com/industries/restaurants) running tight on time.

### Agriculture
icon: Wrench

Equipment depreciation, seasonal income, and crop insurance proceeds complicate a standard ledger. We structure [agriculture](https://www.bblcpa.com/industries/agriculture) books around your harvest cycle, not a generic calendar quarter.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## No surprise bills, ever

Traditional accountants bill by the hour, which means every phone call, every question, every "can you check this real quick" shows up as a line item later. We don't work that way.

Our accounting and bookkeeping service runs on one fixed monthly fee. Call, email, ask a question, none of it changes your invoice. You'll know exactly what you're paying before the month starts, every month, for as long as you're a client.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: port-arthur-business-owner-handshake.jpg | alt: "Business owner shaking hands with an accountant in an office" | query: "business owner accountant handshake office" -->
## Ready to stop doing your own books

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has guided Southeast Texas business owners through their books since 1950, seventy-five years of late nights spent so clients didn't have to spend theirs that way. Whether you run a restaurant in Port Arthur or a clinic in Beaumont, our team can take the bookkeeping off your hands starting this month.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

### Payroll services | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: payroll-handoff-meeting.jpg | alt: "Business owner shaking hands with accountant over payroll paperwork" | query: "small business owner meeting accountant" -->
## Full-service payroll so you can get back to running your business

Payroll is one of those jobs that never quite leaves your desk. Deadlines come whether you are ready or not, tax rules shift, and one missed filing can turn into a penalty letter. That is the part we take off your plate completely.

This is a full handoff, not another software login you have to babysit. We run your payroll through a secure online platform, handle the filings, and keep you compliant so you are never the one chasing a deadline. You stay focused on patients, customers, crews, or whatever actually grows your business.

Business owners across Southeast Texas already trust us to keep their people paid accurately and on time. You get the same steady hand, the same plain answers, and a payroll process that simply works in the background while you run the show.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: payroll-processing-desk.jpg | alt: "Accountant entering payroll details on a laptop at a desk" | query: "accountant processing payroll laptop" -->
## Everything included in your payroll service

Think of this as your whole payroll department, handled by people who do it every day. Here is what we manage for you:

- **Scheduled payroll processing** on the pay cycle you choose, weekly, biweekly, semimonthly, or monthly.
- **Employee setup** for every new hire, including the paperwork and details that keep things compliant from day one.
- **Salary and wage changes** processed cleanly, so raises, bonuses, and rate adjustments show up correctly.
- **Payroll tax filings** prepared and submitted on schedule, federal and state, so deadlines never sneak up on you.
- **Direct deposit** that puts pay in your team's accounts on time, every time.
- **Compliance reporting** that keeps your records straight and audit-ready.

No single piece of this should land back on your desk. We handle the routine runs and the odd situations, like a mid-cycle hire or a correction that needs to happen fast. When a question comes up, you talk to a real person who already knows your account, not a help-line script. The goal is simple: every detail covered, nothing for you to remember.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: predictable-monthly-billing.jpg | alt: "Calendar and invoice on a desk showing a fixed monthly amount" | query: "monthly budget planning desk" -->
## No surprise bills, no hidden fees

Most people brace themselves before opening an invoice from an accounting firm. We took that worry out entirely. Your payroll service is part of a fixed monthly fee, so you know the exact number before the month begins and you see the same number when it ends.

No per-run charges. No add-ons that show up after the fact. No clock running every time you call with a question. One predictable amount, bundled with the rest of your service, so payroll fits your budget instead of surprising it.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: payroll-dashboard-access.jpg | alt: "Business owner viewing payroll reports on a tablet screen" | query: "business owner reviewing reports tablet" -->
## Your payroll data is always within reach

When you want to check a pay run or pull a report, you should not have to call and wait. Log in to the secure online platform and your payroll activity is right there, current and ready.

See what was paid, when it went out, and where your numbers stand, any time of day. That kind of real-time visibility means you are never guessing and never stuck waiting on someone to email you a file. The information is yours, and it is always within reach.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Payroll done right for the businesses we know best

Payroll looks different depending on who you employ. We have handled these situations across Southeast Texas for over 70 years, so the wrinkles in your industry are familiar territory.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Tipped wages, hourly shifts, and high turnover make restaurant payroll its own animal. We keep tip reporting and pay runs accurate so your staff and your books stay square.

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

Multi-provider practices often mean varied pay structures and benefit details. We sort the complexity so your team is paid correctly and your filings hold up.

### Dentists
icon: HeartPulse

A dental practice mixes salaried providers with hourly support staff. We handle both cleanly, including the changes that come with adding a hygienist or associate.

### Agriculture
icon: Home

Seasonal crews and shifting headcounts make farm payroll unpredictable. We scale your pay runs up and down with the season and keep compliance steady throughout.

### Small business owners
icon: Briefcase

Whether you have three employees or thirty, we run payroll so you can spend your time on the work that actually pays the bills.

### New business owners
icon: Lightbulb

Starting out means getting payroll right before the first paycheck goes out. We set it up correctly so you launch on solid footing.

<!-- block: stats-bar | variant: 3-up -->
## Trusted by Southeast Texas businesses since 1950

We opened our doors in Port Arthur in 1950 and have been keeping local businesses, nonprofits, and families on track ever since. Payroll, tax, and accounting support runs year-round here, not just during the end-of-year rush. When you need an answer in June, we pick up the phone.

### 1950

## Ready to hand off your payroll for good

You do not have to keep carrying payroll on your own. Hand it to a team that has run it for Southeast Texas businesses since 1950, and let us guide you toward a healthier financial future, one paycheck at a time. It starts with a simple conversation about how you pay your people today.

### Business tax planning and preparation | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Tax season handled from start to finish

Business tax planning and preparation at Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore covers both ends of the calendar: the strategy that happens months before a number ever lands on a form, and the filing itself. Everything moves through a secure online platform, so you upload documents, review your return, and message your CPA without driving across Port Arthur or printing a single page.

This isn't a service assembled for filing season and forgotten the rest of the year. BBL has prepared returns and built tax strategy for Southeast Texas businesses since 1950, and that history shapes how the firm handles deadlines, deductions, and IRS correspondence today. You get a plan built around your business, not a generic checklist recycled from the last client.

No shoebox of receipts. No waiting on hold. Just a clear process and a team that already knows how Texas businesses get taxed.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: year-round-tax-strategy-meeting.jpg | alt: "CPA and business owner reviewing quarterly tax planning documents at desk" | query: "accountant business owner reviewing documents" -->
## Year-round tax strategy so nothing catches you off guard

Filing a return once a year tells you what already happened. It doesn't change the outcome. BBL's tax service is built around quarterly check-ins and ongoing planning, so estimated payments, entity decisions, and deduction opportunities get addressed while there's still time to act on them, not in March when the prior year is already locked in.

That means a conversation about equipment purchases before year-end, not after. A heads-up on a quarterly estimate before the penalty hits. A second look at your entity structure if your revenue has shifted enough to matter.

This level of attention is already part of the fixed monthly fee, not an add-on billed by the hour every time you call with a question. You won't get a surprise invoice for a fifteen-minute conversation about a 1099 question in October. The planning and the filing are part of the same bundled service, which is the whole point of working with a firm instead of software.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Tax planning built around your industry

A restaurant's tax picture isn't a dentist's tax picture, and a row crop operation has almost nothing in common with either one. Depreciation schedules, entity structure, payroll timing, and deduction strategy all shift depending on what you do for a living, and Southeast Texas has a specific mix of industries that BBL has worked with for decades.

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

Practice income, entity structure, and retirement contributions all interact in ways that benefit from a CPA who understands medical practice economics. [Learn more about physician services](/industries/physicians).

### Dentists
icon: HeartPulse

Equipment purchases, practice transitions, and associate-to-owner shifts each carry different tax implications worth planning around in advance. [See dental practice support](/industries/dentists).

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Tip reporting, razor-thin margins, and high transaction volume call for a tax approach built for food service, not retail. [Explore restaurant tax planning](/industries/restaurants).

### Agriculture
icon: Wrench

Income that swings with weather and commodity prices needs a tax strategy that accounts for the unpredictability built into farming and ranching. [View agriculture services](/industries/agriculture).

### Small business owners
icon: Briefcase

Whatever the industry, owner compensation, deductions, and entity choice get reviewed against what actually makes sense for a business your size. [See small business tax support](/industries/small-business-owners).

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: vertical -->
## How our tax process works

Getting started doesn't require a stack of paperwork on day one. Here's what the relationship looks like once you're on board.

### Onboarding
You meet with a CPA to walk through your business structure, prior returns, and the goals you have for the year ahead.

### Secure document upload
Tax documents, receipts, and statements get uploaded through the online platform instead of emailed back and forth or dropped off in person.

### Review and strategy
Your CPA reviews everything against current tax law and flags opportunities, whether that's a deduction you missed last year or a structural change worth considering.

### Filing
Once the strategy is set, your return gets prepared, reviewed internally, and filed, with you signing off digitally before anything goes to the IRS.

### Ongoing communication
The relationship doesn't end at filing. Quarterly check-ins and year-round access to your CPA keep planning ahead of deadlines instead of behind them.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: audit-protection-irs-letter.jpg | alt: "CPA reviewing an IRS notice letter with a concerned business owner" | query: "accountant reviewing letter document" -->
## Audit protection is included if you ever need it

An IRS letter shows up and most business owners feel their stomach drop a little, even when it's routine. With Audit Protection included, that letter goes straight to your CPA instead of sitting on your desk while you decide what it means.

BBL handles notices, letters, and IRS proceedings directly, at no extra cost beyond your existing fee. You're not buying a separate insurance policy or signing up for another service. It's built into the relationship from day one, which matters most on the one day you actually need it.

[See how Audit Protection works](/what-we-do/audit-protection).

### Personal tax prep and planning | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/personal-income-tax

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Tax season without the stress

Nobody loves doing their taxes. The forms are confusing, deadlines sneak up, and one wrong move can mean penalties or a refund that's smaller than it should be. At Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore, we handle the paperwork, the deadlines, and the strategy behind your personal tax return, so April stops feeling like a countdown to disaster.

We work with individuals, professionals, and small business owners across Southeast Texas who'd rather spend their time running a practice or raising a family than digging through receipts. Our process happens through a secure online platform, with planning conversations that continue all year, not just in March. That's the difference between filing taxes and actually planning for them.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: personal-tax-documents-review.jpg | alt: "Tax preparer reviewing personal tax documents and forms with client" | query: "tax preparer reviewing documents client" -->
## What our personal income tax service covers

Personal tax prep and planning at BBL covers more than filling out a 1040. We start with a clear picture of your income, deductions, and life changes, then build a return that reflects them accurately and a plan that lowers what you owe next year.

This service fits:
- W-2 employees and salaried professionals with stock options, bonuses, or multiple income sources
- Physicians, dentists, and other high-earning professionals managing student loans, retirement contributions, or practice ownership
- Small business owners and new business owners filing personal returns alongside a Schedule C, K-1, or S-corp distribution
- Retirees and individuals managing investment income, required distributions, or rental property
- Families navigating life events such as marriage, a new home, or college expenses

Every return moves through a secure digital platform where you upload documents, message your preparer, and check status without phone tag or a trip downtown. Planning calls happen throughout the year, not just during filing season, so questions about a bonus, a home sale, or a side business get answered before they affect your return.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: tax-planning-meeting-laptop.jpg | alt: "Accountant and client discussing tax planning strategy on a laptop" | query: "accountant client tax planning meeting" -->
## Year-round support means fewer surprises at filing time

Plenty of tax preparers see you once a year, hand you a return, and disappear until next January. That model works fine until you take a new job, sell a rental property, or have a record year in your practice, and then discover in April that you owe far more than expected.

BBL works differently. Throughout the year, we track the moves that affect your taxes and adjust your withholding or estimated payments before they become a problem at filing time. If you're a physician adding a side practice, a restaurant owner reinvesting profits, or someone who simply got a raise, that's a conversation we have in June or September, not a surprise you discover in April.

Our secure online platform gives you real-time visibility into your financial picture between filing seasons, so you're never wondering where things stand. You can see what's been submitted, what's pending, and what we're projecting for your tax liability, all without waiting on a callback.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: port-arthur-texas-storefront.jpg | alt: "Historic downtown Port Arthur Texas street with local businesses" | query: "small town Texas main street" -->
## Serving Southeast Texas families and professionals since 1950

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has prepared personal tax returns in Port Arthur since 1950. Three generations of Southeast Texas families have walked through doors that started as a local accounting practice and grew into a full-service firm covering tax, payroll, and audit work.

That history means we've watched how local industries shape personal finances, from oil and gas employment swings to the cyclical income of agriculture and the practice economics that come with running a dental or medical office. Physicians, restaurant owners, dentists, and small business owners across the region bring us their personal returns for the same reason: continuity matters when your tax situation gets complicated.

Your preparer builds the return, checks it against your prior filings, and flags any deductions or credits worth a closer look before anything is submitted.

### We file and plan ahead
We file electronically and set up your next planning check-in, so estimated payments or withholding changes happen before next April, not during it.

### Audit protection and IRS representation | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/audit-protection

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: irs-letter-review.jpg | alt: "CPA reviewing an IRS notice with a worried small business owner" | query: "accountant reviewing letter with client" -->
## When the IRS contacts you, we step in

An envelope from the IRS lands in your mailbox, and your stomach drops. Take a breath. A notice does not mean you did anything wrong. The IRS sends letters for plenty of routine reasons: a mismatched 1099, a math discrepancy, a deduction that needs backup, or a random selection for review.

Audit protection means you never open that conversation alone. When a notice arrives, you forward it to us and we take over. We read the fine print, figure out what the IRS actually wants, and respond on your behalf. You stay focused on running your practice, your restaurant, or your farm while we handle the back-and-forth. Calm, direct, and on your side: that is how we have answered IRS letters for Southeast Texas clients for decades.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: audit-documents-organized.jpg | alt: "Organized financial documents and folders prepared for an IRS audit response" | query: "organized tax documents folders desk" -->
## What our audit protection service covers

This is full representation, not a hand-holding session where you still do the hard part. We manage the entire lifecycle of an IRS inquiry from the first letter to the final resolution.

- Reading and interpreting every IRS notice and letter so nothing gets missed or misunderstood
- Responding directly to the IRS on your behalf, in writing and by phone, so you are not stuck on hold
- Representing you at all audit proceedings, examinations, and appeals
- Gathering and organizing the documentation the IRS requests, with you sharing files through our secure online platform
- Negotiating outcomes and explaining your options in plain language at every step

Here is the part that matters most during a stressful stretch: there is no meter running. Audit support is built into your fixed monthly fee. No surprise invoices, no billable hours stacking up while you wait for resolution. You already know what you pay each month, and IRS representation is part of it. That predictability is the whole point. A notice is stressful enough without watching the cost climb every time you call us.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: cpa-firm-port-arthur.jpg | alt: "Experienced CPAs discussing a client case in a Southeast Texas office" | query: "experienced accountants discussing case office" -->
## Why having a CPA in your corner makes all the difference

Picture two taxpayers facing the same audit. One goes in solo, guessing at what the examiner is allowed to ask and signing whatever gets put in front of them. The other has a seasoned CPA firm answering every question, citing the tax code, and pushing back when something is off. Those two people get very different outcomes.

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has been doing this since 1950. IRS representation is one of our core specialties, not a sideline we tacked on. Over seven decades in Port Arthur, we have learned how the IRS thinks, what examiners look for, and how to present your records so a routine review stays routine. The agent on the other end of the line talks to people who do this work every day, not someone reading the instructions for the first time. That changes the tone of the whole conversation, and it changes where it ends up.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Who this service is built for

Some businesses simply draw more IRS attention than others. If you work in one of these fields, audit protection belongs in your corner.

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

High income, complex equipment depreciation, and practice entity structures put physicians under a brighter spotlight. We keep your deductions defensible and your records ready.

### Dentists
icon: HeartPulse

Between equipment financing, payroll, and practice ownership, dental practices carry the kind of complexity that invites a closer look. We make sure every number holds up.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Cash handling, tip reporting, and tight margins make food service one of the most audited industries around. We help you document everything the IRS likes to question.

### Agriculture businesses
icon: Home

Seasonal income, equipment deductions, and land valuations create plenty of moving parts. We keep Southeast Texas farms and growers prepared for scrutiny.

### Small business owners
icon: Briefcase

Payroll, contractor 1099s, and home office deductions are common audit triggers. Whatever your industry, we handle the IRS so you can keep running the shop.

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: horizontal -->
## How the process works

Four steps, and we do the heavy lifting on every one.

### You receive an IRS notice
A letter or notice shows up. Do not respond, do not panic, and do not throw it in a drawer. Just get it to us.

### You contact us
Send us the notice and any related paperwork through our secure online platform. We confirm what the IRS is asking and explain what it means for you.

### We take over communication
We respond to the IRS on your behalf, handle the documentation, and represent you at any proceedings. You upload anything we need and watch progress in real time, so you always know where things stand.

### We reach resolution
We see the matter through to the end, walk you through the outcome, and put steps in place to keep it from happening again.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: financial-future-meeting.jpg | alt: "CPA and small business owner shaking hands after a planning meeting" | query: "accountant client handshake meeting" -->
## Start protecting yourself before a notice arrives

The smartest time to set up audit protection is before any letter shows up, not after. Enroll now and you are covered the day a notice lands, with no scramble to find representation and no surprise bill for the help. We have guided Southeast Texas businesses through IRS questions since 1950, and we would be glad to do the same for you. Let us guide you toward a healthy financial future, starting with the peace of mind that comes from knowing you will never face the IRS alone. [Schedule a consultation](/contact)

### Business management services | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Your finances, finally working for your business

Most small business owners in Southeast Texas didn't start their company to become financial analysts. You're filling prescriptions, seating tables, treating patients, or running cattle, not building cash flow models at midnight. But somebody needs to watch the numbers: where the money's going, what's actually profitable, and when a slow season is about to turn into a real problem.

That's the gap Business Management Services fills. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore gives Southeast Texas business owners CFO-level financial oversight, the kind of strategic guidance that used to require hiring a full-time executive or a national consulting firm. We translate the numbers into decisions you can actually act on, then we price it like a service built for a small business, not a Fortune 500 company.

This isn't a side offering. It's the financial command center your business has been missing since day one.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: business-financial-dashboard-review.jpg | alt: "Business owner and accountant reviewing financial dashboard on laptop" | query: "business owner reviewing financial reports laptop" -->
## What our business management services include

Running a business without financial oversight is like driving with the gas gauge covered. You know you're moving, but you have no idea how much runway you've got left. Our Business Management Services give you that gauge back, delivered through the same secure online platform you already use for payroll and tax, all wrapped into one fixed monthly fee.

Here's what's included:

- Regular financial reviews, so you see trends before they become problems
- Cash flow analysis that flags shortfalls weeks before they hit your account
- KPI tracking built around the metrics that actually matter for your business, not generic templates
- Strategic planning guidance for hiring, expansion, equipment purchases, and pricing decisions
- Real-time access to your financial dashboard, anytime, from any device

There's no hourly clock running in the background. No surprise invoice after a planning call. One predictable monthly fee covers the oversight, the analysis, and the access. That's the bundled model this service is built around, and it's why business owners in Port Arthur, Beaumont, and across Southeast Texas use it instead of hiring an in-house CFO.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Who this service is built for

Business management looks different in a restaurant kitchen than it does in a dental office. Here's how the service flexes to fit.

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

Practice overhead creeps up fast: staffing, equipment leases, insurance reimbursements that lag months behind. We track the numbers that determine whether a practice grows or just survives.

### Dentists
icon: ClipboardCheck

Between equipment financing, hygienist payroll, and insurance billing cycles, cash flow gets complicated quickly. We build the financial picture that supports adding a chair or opening a second location.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Restaurants live and die by weekly cash flow. Food cost percentages, labor ratios, and slow-season dips need daily attention, not a once-a-year look at the books.

### Agriculture
icon: MapPin

Seasonal income, equipment debt, and land decisions require planning that spans years, not quarters. We help agriculture operators across Southeast Texas plan around the cycle instead of against it.

### Small Business Owners
icon: Briefcase

Whether you're running a retail shop or a service company, the same question applies: is this business actually making money, and where? We answer it every month.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: port-arthur-cpa-team-history.jpg | alt: "CPA team reviewing documents at a Port Arthur office desk" | query: "accounting team office discussion" -->
## How BBL has guided Southeast Texas businesses since 1950

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore opened its doors in Port Arthur in 1950. Three generations of business owners across Southeast Texas have walked through that door since, from shrimp boat operators to oil field suppliers to today's restaurant and medical practice owners.

That history matters when you're deciding who gets a look at your books. We've watched local industries cycle through booms, hurricanes, and recoveries, and we know what survives.

Business Management Services come wrapped into the same fixed monthly fee model that covers your accounting, payroll, and tax work. One number, billed monthly, no meter running in the background. Ask a question in March about a hiring decision or in October about equipment financing, and it's already covered.

The secure online platform gives you real-time visibility into your financials any day of the year, not just at quarter-end or tax season. You see what we see, when we see it. That transparency is the point: financial guidance only helps if you can act on it the moment you need it, not three weeks later when the invoice clears.

### Business foundation services | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-foundation-services

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Starting a business is exciting, the paperwork, not so much

You've got the idea, the location scouted, maybe a logo already picked out. What you probably haven't sorted out is whether you need an LLC or an S-Corp, what your chart of accounts should look like, or whether a SEP-IRA or a Solo 401(k) makes more sense for a one-person shop. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has walked new owners through exactly this since 1950. Three generations of CPAs have watched businesses open their doors across Southeast Texas, and that history tells us which decisions matter on day one and which can wait until month three. Our business foundation services give you a clear starting point, built by people who've set up hundreds of these structures before, not a generic checklist pulled off the internet.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: new-business-planning-session.jpg | alt: "New business owner and accountant reviewing startup documents together" | query: "new business owner meeting accountant" -->
## What our business foundation services include

Think of this as your launchpad, not a list of separate purchases. Everything below is bundled into one fixed monthly fee, delivered through the same secure online platform you'll use for the life of your business.

- **[Entity type analysis](/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis):** We compare LLC, S-Corp, partnership, and sole proprietor structures against your actual liability exposure and tax situation, not a generic flowchart.
- **[Accounting system setup](/what-we-do/accounting-system-setup):** Your chart of accounts, invoicing, and reporting get built correctly the first time, so nothing needs to be redone at tax season.
- **[Retirement plan analysis](/what-we-do/retirement-plan-analysis):** We research SEP-IRAs, Solo 401(k)s, and SIMPLE plans and tell you which one actually fits your payroll size and cash flow.
- **[Credit card rewards advisory](/what-we-do/credit-card-rewards):** We help you pick a business card that earns toward what you're already spending, instead of one chosen at random at a bank counter.
- **CFO-level guidance:** Once the foundation is set, our [business management services](/what-we-do/business-management-services) keep an experienced eye on your numbers as you grow.

One fee, one dashboard, no surprise invoices for "extra" startup questions.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: entity-structure-consultation.jpg | alt: "CPA explaining business entity options to a new owner at a desk" | query: "accountant explaining documents client desk" -->
## Why your entity structure decision matters more than you think

Pick the wrong entity type and you'll feel it for years, not weeks. A sole proprietor exposes personal assets to business liability. An S-Corp filed too early adds payroll and reasonable-compensation rules a single-owner shop isn't ready for. An LLC taxed the wrong way can mean self-employment tax on income that didn't need to be taxed that way at all.

This is why entity selection comes before the bank account, before the logo gets printed on anything, before the first invoice goes out. Our [entity type analysis](/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis) walks through liability protection, tax treatment, and what changes if you bring on a partner or employee next year. Restaurants, dental practices, and agriculture operations each carry different risk profiles, and the right structure for a physician's solo practice rarely matches what works for a two-person farm operation. Get this right first, and everything else, the books, the payroll, the tax filing, gets simpler.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: small-business-bookkeeping-setup.jpg | alt: "Small business owner viewing financial dashboard on laptop screen" | query: "small business owner laptop financial dashboard" -->
## Get your books right from day one

Most new owners don't think about bookkeeping until April, then scramble to reconstruct a year of transactions from bank statements and memory. We set up your accounting system before you need it, not after.

Your books, payroll, and reporting run through one secure online platform, so you can check cash position, payables, and revenue in real time instead of waiting on a quarterly call. Because everything is bundled into a fixed monthly fee, growth doesn't trigger a surprise bill. Add an employee, open a second location, or start accepting credit cards, your fee stays predictable while we adjust the work behind it. That's the difference between bookkeeping built for a startup and bookkeeping inherited from a spreadsheet nobody updated since launch day.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

New practice owners need entity guidance that accounts for malpractice exposure and group ownership questions before the first patient is seen. [See our physician services](/industries/physicians).

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Tight margins and tip reporting make accurate setup non-negotiable from week one. [See our restaurant services](/industries/restaurants).

### Dentists
icon: HeartPulse

Equipment financing and practice ownership structure both hinge on getting the entity decision right early. [See our dentist services](/industries/dentists).

### Agriculture
icon: Wrench

Land, equipment, and seasonal cash flow call for an entity structure built around how farm income actually moves. [See our agriculture services](/industries/agriculture).

### Small business owners
icon: Briefcase

Whether it's a retail storefront or a service business, the same foundation work applies. [See our small business services](/industries/small-business-owners).

## Ready to build your business on solid ground

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has been part of Port Arthur since 1950, and over 70 years we've learned what new owners actually need versus what they're told they need. ---

### Entity type analysis | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Your business structure shapes everything that comes after

Before you file a single tax return or hire your first employee, one decision sets the tone for every dollar that follows: how you structure your business. Sole proprietorship, partnership, LLC, S-Corp, C-Corp, each comes with its own tax treatment, liability exposure, and paperwork burden, and the right answer depends on your situation, not a generic checklist you found online.

Get it right early and you avoid years of overpaid taxes, exposed personal assets, and awkward conversations with the IRS. Get it wrong, and unwinding it later costs more in legal fees and lost deductions than doing it correctly the first time would have.

At Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore, we treat entity selection as the starting line, not an afterthought. Southeast Texas business owners come to us before they incorporate, not after they've discovered a problem. That's where the smart money gets spent.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: entity-comparison-meeting.jpg | alt: "Accountant explaining business entity options to a client at a desk" | query: "accountant explaining documents to client" -->
## The structures we compare and what they mean for your business

We sit down with your numbers and your goals, then walk through five structures: sole proprietorship, general or limited partnership, LLC, S-Corp, and C-Corp. Each one taxes profit differently, shields or exposes your personal assets differently, and comes with its own filing deadlines and state requirements.

A solo dentist opening a first practice has different needs than three physicians forming a group, and a restaurant adding a second location faces different liability questions than a farm bringing on a family partner. We compare self-employment tax exposure, payroll requirements, deduction eligibility, and the administrative load of each option against your actual plans, not a textbook scenario.

Texas has its own quirks too. Franchise tax thresholds, registered agent rules, and how the state treats LLCs versus corporations all factor into the recommendation. You don't have to research any of it. We've already done the homework, and we'll explain what we found in plain language, no jargon, no guessing which form to file.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: business-risk-analysis.jpg | alt: "Accountant reviewing financial risk documents with a business owner" | query: "accountant reviewing financial risk documents" -->
## How the wrong structure costs you more than you think

A mismatched entity rarely announces itself right away. It shows up later, in a tax bill bigger than it should be, in personal assets exposed to a lawsuit, or in a deal that falls apart because your structure can't accommodate a new partner.

Here's what we see most often:

- A physician operating as a sole proprietor pays self-employment tax on income an S-Corp election could have shielded, often thousands of dollars a year.
- A restaurant owner with one LLC covering multiple locations puts every location's assets at risk if a single location gets sued.
- A new business owner who skips the analysis discovers, mid-negotiation with an investor, that their structure can't issue the equity the deal requires.
- An agriculture operation passed between generations without the right entity in place faces estate and succession headaches that proper planning would have avoided.

None of these mistakes are exotic. They're common, and one conversation before you file is usually enough to avoid them.

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: vertical -->
## How BBL walks you through the decision

We keep the process grounded in your actual business, not abstract theory.

### A conversation about where you're headed
We start with your goals, current revenue, growth plans, and risk tolerance, not a form to fill out alone.

### A look at your specific situation
We review your current structure, or lack of one, your industry, and what's realistic for a dental practice, restaurant, farm, or new venture.

### A plain-language comparison
We lay out two or three viable options side by side, with the tax and liability tradeoffs spelled out in dollars, not jargon.

### A clear recommendation, and help acting on it
You get a specific recommendation, and if you move forward, we handle the paperwork and track everything through your secure online portal, year-round, not just at filing season.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: business-services-planning.jpg | alt: "Small business owner reviewing financial plans with an accountant" | query: "small business owner financial planning meeting" -->
## This service fits naturally with your bigger financial picture

Entity type analysis rarely stands alone. New business owners often pair it with Business Foundation Services to get the books, payroll, and tax registration set up correctly from day one. Once the structure is set, Outsourced Accounting keeps the financials current and tax planning stays proactive instead of reactive every April.

It's all delivered through the same secure online platform, for one fixed monthly fee. No separate invoice for every phone call, no surprise charges when your CPA spends an extra hour on your return. You see what you're paying and what you're getting, every month, for as long as you work with us.

<!-- block: testimonials | variant: grid -->

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore opened its doors in Port Arthur in 1950, and the firm has guided Southeast Texas business owners for more than seventy years since. The clients we serve today, physicians, dentists, restaurant operators, agriculture families, and new business owners just getting started, are the same kinds of clients who walked through our doors decades ago. What's changed is the technology: a secure online platform now puts your financials in front of you in real time, instead of waiting on a quarterly mailed report.

The entity you choose today affects your taxes, your liability, and your options for years down the road. Get it right with a firm that's been guiding Southeast Texas business owners since 1950. ### Accounting system setup | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting-system-setup

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Your books should work for you, not against you

A bookkeeping system that fights you every month costs more than time. Misclassified expenses, bank feeds that don't match, and a chart of accounts built for someone else's business turn tax season into a scramble and make it nearly impossible to know where you actually stand. Across Port Arthur and the rest of Southeast Texas, we see the same pattern again and again: a small business owner inherits a setup from a previous bookkeeper, a software default, or plain trial and error, and nobody ever goes back to fix it. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants builds and repairs accounting systems so the numbers on your screen are the numbers that matter, set up once, configured correctly, and ready to support real decisions.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: accounting-system-cleanup.jpg | alt: "CPA configuring chart of accounts on a desktop computer" | query: "accountant setting up bookkeeping software" -->
## What our accounting system setup service includes

Some businesses need a system built from the ground up. Others have years of tangled data that needs sorting before it's useful for anything, including your taxes. Either way, this is hands-on configuration work, not a one-hour consultation followed by a PDF.

- New system builds in QuickBooks, Xero, or your platform of choice, configured around how your business actually operates
- Cleanup of existing files, including reclassifying transactions, reconciling old accounts, and fixing duplicate entries
- Chart of accounts structured for your industry and your entity type, so reports actually mean something
- Integration checks across bank feeds, payroll, point-of-sale, and merchant processing accounts
- Workflow configuration so recurring transactions, invoices, and bills route to the right place automatically
- Documentation and a walkthrough for your team, so the system stays clean after we hand it back

If you're also restructuring how the business itself is set up, our [entity type analysis](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis) service pairs naturally with a system rebuild.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: real-time-financial-dashboard.jpg | alt: "Business owner viewing financial dashboard on a tablet" | query: "small business owner reviewing financial dashboard" -->
## A clean system pays for itself from day one

A properly built system doesn't just look better. It changes how fast everything downstream moves. Bookkeeping takes less time because transactions land where they belong instead of in a miscellaneous bucket. Financial statements come out accurate the first time, which means fewer surprises when tax season hits. And because the setup connects directly to our secure online platform, you get real-time visibility into cash position, profit, and expenses without waiting on a monthly report.

This is also where the fixed monthly fee model pays off. Setup connects straight into our [outsourced accounting](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting) service, so you're not paying separately every time something needs adjusting. One bundled fee, no billing surprises, and a system built to support it from the start.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Who this service is built for

### Physicians
icon: HeartPulse

Practice owners juggle insurance reimbursements, equipment financing, and multiple revenue streams that generic accounting software was never built to track. We've set up systems that separate clinical and administrative cash flow cleanly. See our [physician services](/industries/physicians) for more.

### Dentists
icon: Stethoscope

Between equipment loans, associate compensation, and supply costs, dental practices need a chart of accounts that goes well beyond the default template. Visit our [dental practice page](/industries/dentists) to learn more.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

High transaction volume, tip reporting, and thin margins mean restaurant books fall out of order fast without the right workflow rules in place. Explore [restaurant accounting](/industries/restaurants).

### Agriculture
icon: Wallet

Seasonal income, equipment depreciation, and land or crop-specific tracking require a system structured around the calendar your business actually runs on. Learn more on our [agriculture page](/industries/agriculture).

### New business owners
icon: Lightbulb

Starting clean is easier than fixing later. New owners often need a system built from nothing, paired with foundational guidance on entity structure and reporting. See [new business owner services](/industries/new-business-owners).

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: small-business-team-meeting.jpg | alt: "Accountant and business owner discussing bookkeeping workflow at a desk" | query: "accountant client meeting office discussion" -->
## Setup is just the start, we stay in your corner

A system that's never maintained drifts back into chaos within a year. That's why setup connects directly into our ongoing [accounting and bookkeeping](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting) service, where the same team that built your system keeps it running month to month. For business owners who want financial oversight without hiring a full-time controller, our [business management services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services) layer CFO-level guidance on top, at a fraction of what an in-house hire would cost. Setup is the foundation. What we build after it is the partnership.

<!-- block: content-prose -->

Seventy-plus years in Port Arthur means we've seen what happens when a system gets built right the first time, and what happens when it doesn't. Our team handles accounting system setup as one piece of a broader practice that includes tax, payroll, audit protection, and business advisory work, all delivered with the same seasoned hands. Businesses change owners, software, and growth stages. The system underneath should hold up through all of it.
