### Retirement plan analysis | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/retirement-plan-analysis

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## The right retirement plan can save you money now and later

A good retirement plan does two jobs at once. It lowers your tax bill this year, and it builds wealth you can actually retire on. Plenty of business owners miss one or both, usually because they set up a plan years ago and never looked at it again, or never set one up at all.

That is money left on the table. The contribution limits, the deduction rules, and the right structure for a physician with three employees look nothing like the right structure for a restaurant owner running solo. If your plan was a default choice from a decade ago, it may be costing you more than it saves.

Our retirement plan analysis fixes that. We look at where you are now, what you want later, and which plan moves you toward both. Then we hand you a clear recommendation, not a stack of brochures.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: retirement-plan-options.jpg | alt: "Business owner reviewing retirement account statements at a desk" | query: "business owner reviewing financial documents" -->
## Not all retirement plans are built the same

There is no single best retirement plan. There are several good ones, and which one fits depends on your income, your entity type, and how many people you employ. Here is the plain-language version.

A SEP-IRA is simple to run and lets you contribute a healthy percentage of income, which suits owners with few or no employees. A SIMPLE IRA works well for small teams that want a modest match without heavy administration. A Solo 401(k) is built for owner-only businesses and often allows the largest contributions at moderate income levels. A defined benefit plan, closer to an old-fashioned pension, can shelter much larger amounts for high-earning owners who want to catch up fast.

Each one carries tradeoffs: contribution caps, paperwork, employee coverage rules, and how much flexibility you get year to year. A high-earning dentist nearing retirement has very different math than a new business owner just turning a profit. The wrong plan caps your savings or buries you in administration. The right one does the opposite.

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: vertical -->
## How BBL researches and compares your options

We do the legwork so you do not have to read the fine print on five different plans. Here is how the analysis works.

### We gather the details that matter

We start with your business entity, your owner income, your number of employees, and your goals. A sole proprietor planning to hire next year needs a different recommendation than an established partnership.

### We model the contribution math

For each viable plan, we calculate what you could contribute, what you could deduct, and what it would cost to administer. Real numbers, based on your books, not generic examples.

### We compare options side by side

You get a clear comparison of the plans that actually fit, with the tradeoffs spelled out in plain language. Contribution limits, employee impact, flexibility, and paperwork, all in one view.

### We give you a recommendation you can act on

We tell you which plan we would choose in your position and why. If you want to move forward, we help you set it up and keep it on track year after year.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: tax-strategy-planning.jpg | alt: "Accountant and client discussing tax planning figures together" | query: "accountant client tax planning meeting" -->
## Retirement planning pairs naturally with tax strategy

Retirement contributions and your tax bill are tied together. Every dollar you put into the right plan can reduce taxable income now, which is why this decision works best as part of year-round tax planning, not a rushed call in April.

We build retirement plan analysis into our broader [tax strategy](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax) so the two work in the same direction. We can see how a larger contribution changes your liability, how it interacts with your entity structure, and whether a plan change makes sense before year-end while there is still time to act. One conversation, one strategy, instead of a retirement decision over here and a tax bill surprise over there.

<!-- block: testimonials | variant: grid -->
## Southeast Texas business owners trust BBL with the details

We have helped small businesses, nonprofits, and professionals across Southeast Texas since 1950. Seven decades of tax, payroll, and advisory work means we have seen how retirement decisions play out over careers, not just tax years. Physicians, dentists, restaurant owners, and agriculture businesses count on us to handle the details they would rather not.

And because our guidance comes through a fixed monthly fee that bundles your services, this analysis is part of what you already pay for. No separate invoice, no billing surprise for asking a good question.

### A note for your review

[Insert client testimonial or Google review here highlighting retirement planning or proactive tax guidance.]

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: financial-future-planning.jpg | alt: "Smiling business owner shaking hands with an accountant in an office" | query: "business owner handshake accountant office" -->
## Start planning for your future today

Let us guide you toward a healthy financial future. We will look at your numbers, compare your real options, and recommend the retirement plan that fits your business and your goals. Everything runs through our secure online platform, with year-round support, so you are never buried in paperwork or waiting until tax season to get answers.

Ready to see what the right plan could save you? [Schedule a consultation](/contact).

### Business credit card rewards advisory | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/credit-card-rewards

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Your spending should be working harder for you

You swipe the company card a dozen times a day. Fuel, supplies, software, a working lunch with a vendor. Every one of those purchases could be earning cash back, points, or travel credit, and for most business owners, that money quietly slips away. The card someone signed up for years ago rarely matches how the business spends today. That is a real leak, not a rounding error. A CPA who already sees your books is positioned to catch it. At Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore, we treat your credit card strategy as part of your financial picture, not an afterthought. Small choices, made on purpose, add up across a year.

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: vertical -->
## How BBL's credit card rewards advisory works

This is advisor-led guidance from the firm that keeps your books, not a pitch for a card we get paid to sell.

### We map your spending categories
Using the numbers already in your accounting records, we group your purchases by category, so the comparison reflects how your business operates instead of a one-size-fits-all profile.

### We compare the card options
We research current business cards across rewards structures, annual fees, sign-up bonuses, and redemption rules, then narrow the field to the few that genuinely fit your numbers.

### We recommend and explain the trade-offs
You get a clear recommendation with the reasoning behind it. No commissions, no quotas, no product we are paid to push. Just a straight answer about which card returns the most for the way you spend, and what each one costs you to carry.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: restaurant-owner-card-payment.jpg | alt: "Restaurant owner reviewing card statements at the counter of a busy kitchen" | query: "restaurant owner reviewing receipts" -->
## Rewards that fit how your business spends

A restaurant owner's card statement looks nothing like a physician's, and neither one resembles a farm operation in Southeast Texas. The restaurant runs heavy on food costs, supplies, and utilities. The dental practice spends on lab work, equipment, and staffing. The farm has fuel, feed, and seasonal swings that spike in spring and quiet down by winter.

A generic "best rewards card" list ignores all of that. The card that earns the most for a clinic with steady monthly software and payroll charges may return almost nothing for a grower whose biggest outlays land in two months. We build the recommendation around your categories, your typical monthly volume, and your goals, whether that is cash back to reinvest or travel credit you will actually use. The point is a card that pays you for the spending you were already doing.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: cpa-reviewing-business-finances.jpg | alt: "CPA reviewing financial statements and a credit card with a small business client" | query: "accountant reviewing finances with client" -->
## Why your CPA is the right person to give this advice

Credit card strategy is not a standalone topic. It touches your taxes, your bookkeeping, and your monthly close. A bank or a points blog cannot see any of that. We can.

The annual fee on a business card is generally deductible, which changes the real math on whether a premium card earns its keep. The card you choose also feeds straight into your accounting system, so a clean rewards structure and good digital statements keep your books tidy, while a messy one creates reconciliation headaches every month. We know which cards integrate cleanly because we are the ones reconciling them.

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has worked with small businesses across Southeast Texas since 1950. Seven decades of bookkeeping for restaurants, medical practices, farms, and new owners means we have seen which card choices help and which quietly cause problems at tax time.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: bundled-accounting-services.jpg | alt: "Business owner viewing financial dashboard on a laptop in an office" | query: "business owner laptop financial dashboard" -->
## Part of a bundled approach to your financial health

Credit card guidance is one piece of a full-service relationship, not a line item you pay extra for. When you work with us on a fixed monthly fee, this kind of advice comes with the territory.

- Fixed monthly fee with no surprise invoices for a quick question
- A secure online platform that gives you real-time visibility into your numbers
- [Outsourced accounting](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting) that keeps your books current and reconciled
- [Business management services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services) with CFO-level guidance at a fraction of the cost
- Year-round tax support, so card decisions and deductions stay connected

The credit card recommendation is stronger because we already know your whole financial picture. That is the advantage of bundling instead of buying one-off advice.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: southeast-texas-business-meeting.jpg | alt: "Business owner shaking hands with an accountant across a desk in a local office" | query: "business owner meeting accountant handshake" -->
## Get a credit card strategy that pays you back

If the card in your wallet is earning less than it should, let's fix it. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has guided Port Arthur and Southeast Texas business owners since 1950, with secure online tools and year-round support built in. Bring your questions about rewards, fees, or how a new card fits your books, and we will give you a straight answer grounded in your actual numbers. [Schedule a consultation](/contact) and put your everyday spending to work.

## Industries we serve

### Accounting and tax services for physicians
URL: /industries/physicians

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Your practice deserves a financial team that speaks your language

You trained for years to care for patients, not to reconcile bank statements at 11 p.m. or guess whether your entity structure is costing you money. A medical practice is a complicated business: payroll, insurance reimbursements, overhead, and tax deadlines all run in the background while you see patients.

That background work is where we come in. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has kept the books for small businesses and professionals across Southeast Texas since 1950, and we know what a physician's financials look like before you finish describing them. From our office in Port Arthur, we handle the accounting, payroll, and tax side of your practice so your attention stays where it belongs. Healthy patients. Healthy practice. We help with the second part.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: physician-practice-finances.jpg | alt: "Physician reviewing practice financial reports with an accountant at a desk" | query: "doctor reviewing financial documents office" -->
## What makes accounting for physicians different from general bookkeeping

Most bookkeeping templates assume one revenue stream and a simple expense list. A practice does not work that way.

Your income arrives from insurers, patients, and sometimes ancillary services, each on its own schedule. Insurance reimbursements land weeks after the work, often at a different amount than billed, which makes cash flow harder to read than a typical retail ledger. Your overhead carries clinical staff, malpractice premiums, equipment leases, and supply costs that general bookkeepers rarely categorize correctly.

Then there is structure. Many Texas physicians operate as a PLLC or elect S-corp treatment, and that choice changes how you pay yourself, how much self-employment tax you owe, and what you can deduct. Get it wrong, and you overpay quietly for years.

There are also questions a generalist will not think to ask. How should you handle owner draws across partners in a group practice? Are your retirement contributions optimized against your marginal rate? Is your malpractice coverage recorded in a way that holds up under scrutiny?

We answer those questions because we have answered them before, for dentists, physicians, and other professionals throughout Southeast Texas. Bookkeeping records what happened. Accounting for a practice explains what it means and what to do next.

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## How BBL handles the financial side of your practice

We bundle the services a practice actually needs into one fixed monthly fee. No hourly meter, no surprise invoice after a phone call, and real-time numbers you can check any day through our secure online platform.

### Outsourced accounting
icon: Calculator

A fully bundled accounting service delivered through a secure online platform, billed at one predictable monthly rate. You see your practice's financial picture in real time instead of waiting for a quarterly catch-up.

[Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting)

### Bookkeeping
icon: FileText

Comprehensive bookkeeping handled by a seasoned team, including transaction classification built around how a medical practice earns and spends. Reimbursements, overhead, and payroll all land in the right place.

[Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting)

### Payroll
icon: Users

Full-service payroll for your clinical and administrative staff, run through a secure online platform. Filings, withholdings, and pay runs stay current without you touching a spreadsheet.

[Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll)

### Business tax planning and preparation
icon: Receipt

Strategic tax planning and preparation delivered digitally, with decisions made across the year rather than scrambled together in April. Your practice keeps more of what it earns.

[Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax)

### Business management services
icon: ChartLine

CFO-level financial oversight at a fraction of the cost of a full-time hire, including regular financial reviews and guidance on the decisions that move your practice forward.

[Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services)

### Audit protection
icon: ShieldCheck

Expert IRS representation that handles notices, letters, and proceedings on your behalf. If a question comes from the IRS, it comes to us first.

[Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/audit-protection)

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: physician-tax-planning.jpg | alt: "Accountant and physician discussing tax strategy over financial paperwork" | query: "tax planning meeting professional" -->
## Tax strategy built around how physicians earn and grow

A once-a-year tax preparer records history. We plan ahead, because the choices that lower a physician's tax bill happen during the year, not after it closes.

- **Entity selection that fits your income.** We run the numbers on S-corp, LLC, and PLLC treatment so your structure matches how much you earn and how you pay yourself, not a one-size template.
- **Retirement plan analysis.** We compare plan options, from SEP-IRAs to defined benefit setups, so high earners shelter income at the right rate. [See how we analyze retirement plans](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/retirement-plan-analysis).
- **Deduction optimization.** Equipment, continuing education, malpractice premiums, and home office costs get captured correctly instead of left on the table.
- **Year-round support.** You can call in October about a major purchase or a partnership change and get an answer before the decision, included in your monthly fee.
- **Entity type analysis on demand.** As your practice grows, your ideal structure can change. [We revisit it with you](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis).

Proactive beats reactive every time your money is involved. We would rather save you tax in March than explain it next April.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: new-practice-southeast-texas.jpg | alt: "Physician opening a new medical practice reviewing startup plans" | query: "doctor new medical practice opening" -->
## Starting or expanding your practice in Southeast Texas

Opening your own practice, or adding a second location, is one of the biggest financial moves of a physician's career. The wrong setup early creates cleanup work for years.

Our Business Foundation Services help new physician owners launch on solid footing: choosing the right entity, setting up your accounting system so the books are clean from day one, and building a plan for payroll and tax obligations before the first patient walks in. If you are already established and growing, we adjust the structure to match your new scale.

Start with [Business Foundation Services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-foundation-services) and [Entity Type Analysis](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis), and we will map the financial foundation together before you commit to a lease or a hire.

<!-- block: stats-bar | variant: 3-up -->
## Why physicians across Southeast Texas trust Brammer, Begnaud, and Lattimore

Longevity is its own credential. We have prepared taxes and managed books for Southeast Texas businesses through every tax law change since the Truman administration, and physicians stay with us because the numbers are clear, the fee never surprises them, and someone answers when the IRS calls.

### 1950
The year we opened in Port Arthur, serving Southeast Texas ever since.

### 1 fixed fee
Bundled services billed at one predictable monthly rate, no hourly surprises.

### Full IRS representation

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: healthy-practice-finances.jpg | alt: "Physician shaking hands with a financial advisor in a bright office" | query: "doctor handshake professional meeting" -->
## Ready to get your practice finances off your plate

Hand us the books, the payroll, and the tax planning, and check your numbers any time through our secure platform. One monthly fee, no billing surprises, and a team that has guided Southeast Texas practices toward a healthy financial future since 1950. Let us take the financial weight so you can get back to your patients.

### Accounting and tax services for dental practices
URL: /industries/dentists

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: dental-practice-finances.jpg | alt: "Dentist reviewing practice financial reports with an accountant at a desk" | query: "dentist reviewing financial documents office" -->
## Your practice deserves more than a generalist accountant

Running a dental practice means watching two numbers that rarely match: what you produce and what you collect. Add associate pay structures, hygienist hours, and a six-figure CEREC machine depreciating on your books, and the math gets complicated fast. A general accountant who treats your office like any other storefront tends to miss the details that decide whether a strong production month turns into a strong profit month.

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has worked with Southeast Texas businesses since 1950. From our base in Port Arthur, the firm serves dentists, physicians, restaurants, and agriculture operations across the region, so production-versus-collections gaps and slow insurance reimbursement timing are familiar territory here.

That dental focus changes the conversation. Instead of explaining how your office works every spring, you get a [dental practice accountant in Port Arthur, TX](/industries/dentists) who already speaks the language of fee schedules, write-offs, and multi-provider schedules. Less translation, more guidance toward a healthier bottom line.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: dental-office-bookkeeping.jpg | alt: "Bookkeeper entering dental practice transactions on a laptop in a clinic" | query: "bookkeeper laptop medical office" -->
## Accounting and bookkeeping built around how dental offices run

Your books should tell you which procedures drive your margins, not just hand you a year-end lump sum. The BBL team classifies revenue by procedure category, reconciles insurance reimbursements against what you actually billed, and tracks supply and lab costs so you can see exactly where the money goes. Every number lives on a secure online platform, so you get real-time financial visibility instead of waiting on a quarterly packet.

Here is what dentist bookkeeping in Port Arthur covers under one roof:

- Procedure revenue tracked by category, so hygiene, restorative, and ortho each tell their own story
- Insurance reimbursement reconciliation that catches underpayments and slow payers
- Supply, lab, and equipment cost monitoring tied to your production
- Real-time dashboards on a secure online platform you can check from the operatory or home
- Monthly financials reviewed by a seasoned team, not auto-generated and forgotten
- A fully bundled, fixed monthly fee with no billing surprises

That fixed monthly fee is the part most dentists appreciate most. You know the cost before the work starts, and asking a question never triggers a surprise invoice. Explore the full [accounting and bookkeeping service](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting) or the bundled [outsourced accounting option](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting) built for owners who want to hand off the whole back office.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: dental-tax-planning.jpg | alt: "Dentist and CPA discussing tax planning documents in a bright office" | query: "tax planning meeting professional office" -->
## Tax planning and preparation that keeps more money in your practice

April should be a formality, not a fire drill. Most dentists overpay because nobody planned ahead, not because the rules are unfair. A new chair, a digital scanner, or a CBCT unit can often be written off in the year you buy it through Section 179 and bonus depreciation, but only if someone times the purchase and documents it correctly.

BBL handles dental office tax preparation in Port Arthur as a year-round conversation. The team models how your practice structure affects your tax bill, looks at whether an S corporation election fits a solo or group setup, and flags equipment and retirement moves before the calendar closes the door on them. You see the projections through a clean digital experience, sign where you need to, and skip the shoebox-of-receipts ritual.

This is the difference between a filer and a planner. A reactive preparer records what already happened. A proactive [tax planning and preparation team](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax) helps shape what happens next, so dental tax services in Port Arthur, TX actually lower what you owe instead of just reporting it. Year-round access means questions in July get the same attention as questions in March.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: dental-payroll-staff.jpg | alt: "Dental office manager processing staff payroll on a secure computer platform" | query: "office manager payroll computer" -->
## Payroll, compliance, and the back-office work you should not be doing yourself

A dental practice payroll is rarely simple. Hygienists on hourly schedules, associates on production splits, front-desk staff with overtime, and a rotating cast of part-timers all hit the same payroll run. Get the withholding or the classification wrong, and the penalties land on you.

BBL runs full-service dentist payroll in Port Arthur, TX through a secure online platform that handles direct deposit, multi-provider scheduling, and federal and state payroll tax filings. Compensation tied to production gets calculated correctly, on time, every cycle, so your team trusts their paychecks and you stop reconciling them at midnight.

Compliance worries get handled too. If the IRS sends a notice, letter, or audit request, BBL's [audit protection service](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/audit-protection) provides expert representation and deals with the agency directly. You keep treating patients while the correspondence gets answered by people who do this for a living. See the full [payroll service](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll) for everything covered under your fixed monthly fee.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: dental-practice-cfo-guidance.jpg | alt: "Dentist reviewing growth projections and retirement plan options with a financial advisor" | query: "financial advisor charts professional meeting" -->
## CFO-level guidance to help your practice grow on your terms

You trained to fix teeth, not to read cash flow statements or compare retirement plan structures. That gap is exactly where a financial co-pilot earns their keep. BBL's [business management services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services) deliver CFO-level oversight at a fraction of the cost of a full-time hire, including regular financial reviews, cash flow analysis, and benchmarking against where you want the practice to go.

Retirement is a big piece of that picture for dentists. A solo owner and a three-partner group call for very different answers, and the right plan can shelter serious income. The [retirement plan analysis service](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/retirement-plan-analysis) compares SEP IRAs, SIMPLE plans, and defined benefit options so dentist retirement planning in Port Arthur, TX matches your age, income, and timeline rather than a generic template.

Structure matters too. As your practice grows or adds associates, an [entity type analysis](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis) checks whether your current setup still fits, weighing liability, tax treatment, and how you eventually want to exit. Dental practice management accounting in Port Arthur should feel less like guessing and more like reading a map you helped draw.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: new-dental-practice-setup.jpg | alt: "New dentist opening practice reviewing startup checklist with an accountant" | query: "new business owner planning startup office" -->
## Starting a dental practice in Southeast Texas? We help you build it right.

Opening your own office in Port Arthur is exciting and a little overwhelming. The clinical side you have handled. The entity selection, the QuickBooks setup, the first payroll run, and the equipment financing decisions are a different kind of pressure.

BBL's [business foundation services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-foundation-services) walk new owners through launch in plain language: choosing the right structure, registering correctly, and building a financial system that scales. The [accounting system setup](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting-system-setup) gets your books configured cleanly from day one, or cleans up a system someone else left tangled.

Start organized and the next five years get a lot easier. Dental practice setup in Port Arthur done right means fewer surprises, accurate numbers, and a team that already knows your story when tax season arrives.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: dental-practice-consultation.jpg | alt: "Dentist shaking hands with a CPA across a desk in a Port Arthur office" | query: "professional handshake consultation office" -->
## Ready to hand off the financial side of your practice?

Since 1950, dentists and small business owners across Southeast Texas have leaned on Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore to take the books, payroll, and taxes off their plate. You get a fixed monthly fee with no surprises, a secure platform for real-time numbers, and year-round support that does not vanish on April 16.

Bring us your production reports and your questions. The team handles the rest, all year, so you can focus on patients instead of paperwork.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

### Accounting and payroll services for restaurants
URL: /industries/restaurants

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: restaurant-owner-paperwork.jpg | alt: "Restaurant owner reviewing receipts and invoices at a dining table" | query: "restaurant owner reviewing finances" -->
## Running a restaurant is hard enough without the bookkeeping headaches

You didn't sign a lease and build a menu so you could spend your nights matching receipts to a bank statement. Restaurants run on thin margins, and a few percentage points in either direction decide whether the month was good or just busy. Tip reporting alone can eat an evening. Labor costs swing with the season, the weather, and who called in sick. Food prices move every week.

Most owners in Port Arthur and across Southeast Texas are doing the books after close, half asleep, hoping nothing slipped through. That is no way to run a kitchen or a business. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore takes the accounting, payroll, and tax work off your plate completely, so the numbers are handled by people who do this every day while you handle the floor.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: restaurant-accounting-platform.jpg | alt: "Restaurant manager checking financial figures on a laptop in a cafe" | query: "restaurant manager laptop bookkeeping" -->
## How BBL's restaurant accounting package works

Our [outsourced accounting](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting) for restaurants runs on one fixed monthly fee. You know the number before the month starts, and it does not change because tax season arrived or you called with a question. For a business with revenue that climbs on Friday and dips on Tuesday, that kind of predictability is rare and worth a lot.

Everything is bundled and delivered through a secure online platform, so there are no hourly surprises and no separate invoices stacking up. Here is what your monthly fee covers:

- **Bookkeeping** handled by a seasoned team, including transaction classification and monthly reconciliations, so your books are always current and accurate.
- **Payroll** run through a secure platform every pay period, including tipped wages and tax filings.
- **Tax planning and preparation** worked year-round, not crammed into April.
- **Real-time financial access** through your online portal, so food cost and labor numbers are a login away.
- **A single point of contact** who knows your restaurant and answers when you reach out.

The model is simple on purpose. One fee, one platform, one team. You stop guessing what the bill will be and stop wondering whether the books got done. They did. Want the full picture of what bundled service includes? Start on our [services](/services) page or just [schedule a consultation](https://www.bblcpa.com/contact).

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: restaurant-staff-payroll.jpg | alt: "Restaurant servers and kitchen staff working during a busy shift" | query: "restaurant staff working shift" -->
## Payroll for restaurants done right, every pay period

Restaurant payroll is its own animal. You have tipped employees, tip credits, and tipped minimum wage rules that have to line up correctly or you risk a wage claim. Overtime stacks up fast during a rush. Turnover means you are onboarding new hires and issuing final checks more often than most businesses ever do. Miss a detail and it shows up as a penalty or an unhappy server.

Our [full-service payroll](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll) handles all of it through a secure online platform. We calculate tipped and standard wages, track overtime, file your payroll taxes, and keep you compliant with the rules that apply to food service employers. New hire this week and a departure next week? We process both without you chasing paperwork.

Your staff gets paid accurately and on time, every period. You get clean records and one less thing to worry about before doors open.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: restaurant-tax-planning.jpg | alt: "Accountant and restaurant owner discussing tax documents together" | query: "accountant restaurant tax planning" -->
## Tax planning and preparation built around your restaurant's calendar

Tax work for a restaurant should not be a once-a-year scramble. We plan around your year, with quarterly estimates so you are setting money aside before the bill arrives instead of after. That smooths out the cash flow shocks that sink so many food service businesses.

Food service comes with deductions that get missed when someone unfamiliar handles the return. Kitchen equipment and depreciation. Spoiled and wasted inventory. Uniforms and laundry. Smallwares replaced over and over. We know where these live and we claim them. See how our [business tax](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax) and [personal income tax](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/personal-income-tax) work fits together for owners who take income from the business.

If the IRS ever sends a notice, you are covered. Our [audit protection](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/audit-protection) means we handle the letters, the responses, and the representation. You forward the envelope and get back to work. The whole experience runs through the same secure digital workflow as the rest of your service, so nothing gets lost between you and your accountant.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: restaurant-financial-dashboard.jpg | alt: "Restaurant owner viewing financial charts on a tablet at the bar" | query: "business owner financial dashboard tablet" -->
## Real financial visibility so you always know where your restaurant stands

Guessing is expensive. When you cannot see your food cost percentage until the accountant sends something next month, you are making decisions blind. Our secure online platform gives you real-time access to the numbers that actually run a restaurant: food cost as a percent of sales, labor ratios, and cash flow you can read at a glance.

This is the kind of insight a full-time CFO would give you, without the full-time salary. Through our [business management services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services), you get regular financial reporting and a read on the trends that matter, at a fraction of the cost of hiring someone in house. Notice labor creeping up two weeks into the month, and you can adjust the schedule before it costs you. That is the difference between reacting and steering.

<!-- block: content-prose | variant: standard -->
## Serving Southeast Texas restaurants since 1950

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has worked with Southeast Texas businesses since 1950. Seven decades in Port Arthur means we know this community, its independent kitchens, and its family-owned spots that have fed the same neighborhoods for generations. We have seen the local economy through good years and hard ones, and we have helped small operators stay solvent through both.

Here is what you can count on:

- **A fixed monthly fee** with no billing surprises. The number you agree to is the number you pay.
- **A secure online platform** for your books, payroll, and real-time numbers.
- **Year-round support** from a team that knows your restaurant by name, not a seasonal hotline.

Ready to see how it fits your kitchen? [Schedule a consultation](https://www.bblcpa.com/contact) and we will walk you through it.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: restaurant-kitchen-chef.jpg | alt: "Chef plating a dish in a busy restaurant kitchen" | query: "chef plating dish restaurant kitchen" -->
## Ready to hand off the numbers and get back to your kitchen

### Accounting and advisory services for agriculture businesses
URL: /industries/agriculture

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Southeast Texas farms and ag operations deserve a CPA who gets it

Farming income rarely arrives in tidy monthly chunks. A crop sells, a herd goes to market, an input bill lands right before planting, and the cash flow chart looks more like a rollercoaster than a steady line. That seasonality is normal for agriculture, and it shapes everything from your tax timing to your equipment purchases.

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants has worked alongside Southeast Texas businesses since 1950. Over seven decades in Port Arthur, we have learned how commodity price swings, deferred income, and weather-driven uncertainty change the way a farm or ranch should keep its books. You run the operation. We handle the accounting, payroll, and tax work behind it, in plain language and on a schedule that respects your busy seasons.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: farm-accounting-records.jpg | alt: "Farmer reviewing financial records on a laptop near agricultural equipment" | query: "farmer reviewing finances on farm" -->
## Accounting built around how ag businesses make money

General bookkeeping software treats a corn sale and a consulting invoice the same way. Your operation deserves better. We track crop and livestock income separately, so you can see which lines of your business are carrying the load and which are dragging. Equipment depreciation gets handled correctly the first time, including the heavy machinery purchases that drive much of an ag balance sheet.

Input costs, seed, feed, fertilizer, fuel, and labor get categorized so you can compare them season over season instead of guessing. Land transactions, leases, and improvements are recorded with the detail your lender and your future self will both appreciate.

Our [outsourced accounting](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting) service bundles all of this into one fixed monthly fee. No hourly meter running every time you call with a question, no surprise invoice after a busy quarter. You know what you pay each month, and you watch your numbers update in real time through a secure online platform. When the banker asks for a current profit and loss statement, it is already there.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: harvest-tax-planning.jpg | alt: "Combine harvester working a field during harvest season" | query: "combine harvester field harvest" -->
## Tax planning that works with your harvest schedule, not against it

Ag taxes have their own rulebook, and used well, those rules put real money back in your pocket. We plan year-round, not just in April, so the moves that matter happen while there is still time to make them.

- **Section 179 and bonus depreciation** timed to the years a deduction does the most good, especially after a major equipment purchase
- **Farm income averaging** to smooth out a high-income year against leaner ones and lower your overall tax bill
- **Estimated tax timing** mapped to when your income actually arrives, so you are not overpaying in spring and scrambling in fall
- **Entity structure choices** that reduce self-employment tax and keep more of what you earn
- **Deferred grain and livestock sale strategies** coordinated with your accounting records so the tax treatment matches the transaction

This is the work behind our [tax planning and preparation](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax) service. We address the complexity head-on instead of handing you a return in April and hoping for the best. If a notice ever shows up, our [audit protection](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/audit-protection) service puts an experienced CPA between you and the IRS.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: farm-payroll-workers.jpg | alt: "Agricultural workers in a field during the growing season" | query: "agricultural workers field crew" -->
## Payroll and compliance handled so you can stay in the field

Ag labor is rarely simple. You might run a small year-round crew and then triple your headcount for harvest, with a mix of W-2 employees and 1099 contractors moving through in a matter of weeks. Each one carries filing requirements, and the deadlines do not pause for planting season.

Our [full-service payroll](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll) management runs through a secure online platform, covering wage calculations, tax withholding, and the year-end W-2 and 1099 paperwork. Seasonal spikes get handled without you chasing forms between rows. You approve, we process, and the compliance side stays clean.

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 2-col -->
## CFO-level guidance without the full-time CFO price tag

Growing an ag operation brings decisions that a tax return alone cannot answer. Should you buy or lease that next parcel? How does adding a packing line change your cash flow? When does it make sense to bring the next generation into ownership? A full-time CFO can guide those calls, but the salary is out of reach for most family operations. We give you that level of insight at a fraction of the cost.

### Business management services
icon: ChartLine

Regular financial oversight, performance reviews, and forward-looking guidance so you make decisions about land, equipment, and expansion with real numbers in front of you. [See how it works](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services).

### Entity type analysis
icon: Scale

The right structure affects your self-employment tax, your liability, and how easily you can pass the operation to the next generation. We compare your options and explain the trade-offs in plain terms. [Explore entity analysis](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis).

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: southeast-texas-farmland.jpg | alt: "Open Southeast Texas farmland under a wide sky" | query: "texas farmland rural landscape" -->
## Trusted by Southeast Texas since 1950

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has kept the books, filed the returns, and answered the late-season questions for Port Arthur and Southeast Texas businesses for over seventy years. That history means we have seen the lean years and the record harvests, and we know how to plan for both. When the IRS sends a letter, our audit protection service handles the notices and proceedings so you stay focused on the operation.

Let us guide you toward a healthy financial future. [Schedule a consultation](/contact) and tell us about your operation.

### Accounting and advisory services for small business owners
URL: /industries/small-business-owners

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Running a small business is hard enough without the bookkeeping headaches

Running a restaurant in Port Arthur or a dental practice in Beaumont leaves little time for reconciling bank statements or chasing a missing invoice. Most small business owners we meet are reacting to their finances instead of managing them: a tax bill shows up without warning, a payroll glitch eats an afternoon, and nobody can say with certainty what the business actually made last month.

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore takes that weight off your desk. We handle the bookkeeping, payroll, and tax work so you can spend your time on customers, patients, or crops instead of spreadsheets. You started this business to do the work you're good at. Let us handle the numbers behind it.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: port-arthur-small-business-history.jpg | alt: "Accountant reviewing historical client ledgers in an office setting" | query: "accountant office historical records small town" -->
## More than 70 years of serving Southeast Texas small businesses

Founded in 1950, our firm has worked out of Port Arthur for more than seven decades, long enough to have prepared taxes for three generations of the same family business and to have walked agriculture operations in Jefferson County through hurricane seasons and commodity swings. We've watched local restaurants change hands, dental practices expand, and new businesses open their doors for the first time, often with our help.

That history is institutional memory, and it's useful to you in a practical way. A large national firm rotates you through a revolving cast of associates who don't know Southeast Texas. Here, the people on your account know the local economy, the local tax authorities, and likely a few of your competitors down the street. That's not a sales pitch, it's just how a firm built since 1950 ends up operating.

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Everything your business needs, bundled into one predictable monthly fee

No invoice surprises, no per-task billing, no guessing what next month's accounting bill will look like. Outsourced Accounting bundles the services below into one fixed monthly fee, delivered through a secure online platform where you can check your numbers any time, not just when we call with a report.

### Bookkeeping
icon: Calculator

Transaction classification, account reconciliation, and monthly financial statements handled by a seasoned team, so the numbers are accurate before they ever reach your inbox.

### Payroll
icon: Users

Full-service payroll run through the same secure platform, covering pay runs, tax filings, and compliance, so payday never becomes a fire drill.

### Tax
icon: FileText

Strategic business tax planning and preparation built into your monthly fee rather than billed as a separate, surprise project every spring.

### Business management
icon: ChartLine

Regular financial reporting and CFO-level oversight, so you know where the business stands before a decision has to be made, not after.

One fee. One login. One team that already knows your business when you call.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: small-business-tax-planning-meeting.jpg | alt: "Business owner and accountant reviewing tax documents together" | query: "tax planning meeting small business owner" -->
## Tax support that works all year, not just in April

A lot of small business owners only hear from their accountant during tax season, which means the planning that would have lowered the bill already happened months ago, without them. We work the calendar differently. Tax planning happens in the spring, the summer, and the fall, so by the time a return is filed, there are no surprises left to find.

If the IRS sends a notice anyway, Audit Protection puts a CPA between you and the correspondence. We handle the letters, the documentation, and the proceedings directly, at no extra charge beyond your monthly fee. That's the difference between a firm that disappears after April 15 and one that stays on the phone with the IRS in October if it needs to.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: cfo-advisory-business-owner.jpg | alt: "Small business owner reviewing financial reports with an advisor" | query: "business advisor reviewing financial reports" -->
## CFO-level guidance without the CFO-level cost

A full-time CFO costs more than most small businesses can justify, but the decisions a CFO handles, cash flow, growth timing, entity structure, retirement planning, still have to get made. Business Management Services puts that level of financial oversight on your team at a fraction of the cost of a hire.

In practice, that means:

- Cash flow forecasting that flags a problem before it becomes an emergency
- Growth planning built on your actual numbers, not a guess
- Entity structure review through Entity Type Analysis, so you're not overpaying in taxes because of how the business is set up
- Retirement plan options compared side by side, so you pick the one that fits a small business budget

It's the strategic side of accounting, the part most bookkeeping services never touch.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: new-business-owner-launch-planning.jpg | alt: "New business owner reviewing startup paperwork with a consultant" | query: "new business owner consultation paperwork" -->
## Starting a business in Southeast Texas? We help you build it right from day one

The choices you make in the first few months, entity type, accounting setup, how payroll gets structured, are expensive to undo later. Business Foundation Services and Entity Type Analysis walk new owners through those decisions before they're locked in, with the tax and liability consequences spelled out in plain terms.

We're not just here to file your paperwork. We're here to make sure the foundation under the business can hold the weight of growth. If you're opening your doors for the first time, our [new business owners](/industries/new-business-owners) page covers what that looks like in more detail.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: port-arthur-business-handshake.jpg | alt: "Accountant and small business owner shaking hands after a consultation" | query: "business handshake office consultation" -->
## Ready to hand off the numbers and get back to your business?

Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has worked with small business owners across Southeast Texas since 1950, from restaurants in Port Arthur to dental practices and agriculture operations throughout the region. "They caught a payroll error our last bookkeeper missed for two years," one client told us, the kind of thing a fixed-fee, real-time platform is built to prevent.

Let us guide you toward a healthy financial future. [Schedule a consultation](/contact) and find out what a fixed monthly fee, a secure online platform, and a CPA firm that's been doing this since 1950 can take off your plate.

### Startup accounting and advisory services for new business owners
URL: /industries/new-business-owners

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Starting a business is exciting, the paperwork, not so much

You had an idea worth chasing. What you did not sign up for was a stack of forms asking you to pick an entity type, register for payroll taxes, and set up books before you have sold a thing. That part feels less like running a business and more like decoding a foreign language.

That is where we come in. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has guided Southeast Texas business owners since 1950, and we have walked plenty of first-timers from "I think I want to start something" to "my books are clean and my taxes are handled." You focus on the work you love. We will keep the financial foundation steady underneath it.

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 2-col -->
## How we help new business owners build on solid financial ground

Most new owners do not need ten different vendors. They need a few core services that fit together and one fixed monthly fee so the bill never catches them off guard. Here is the bundle we build around startups in Port Arthur and across Southeast Texas.

### Business Foundation Services
icon: Building2

Startup advisory that gets your finances pointed in the right direction from day one. We help you set up the structure, accounts, and processes a new business needs before the first invoice goes out, so you are not patching mistakes later.

### Entity Type Analysis
icon: Scale

LLC, S-Corp, or sole proprietor? Each choice carries real tax and liability consequences. We walk you through the options based on your goals and your numbers, then help you file the paperwork to make it official.

### Accounting System Setup
icon: Cog

Whether you are starting fresh or cleaning up a system that grew sideways, we configure your bookkeeping so transactions flow correctly and your reports actually mean something. No more guessing whether a number is right.

### Outsourced Accounting
icon: Calculator

Fully bundled bookkeeping, payroll, and tax support delivered through a secure online platform, all for one fixed monthly fee. No surprise invoices, no hourly meter running every time you have a question.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: entity-structure-decision.jpg | alt: "Accountant explaining business structure options to a new business owner" | query: "accountant advising new business owner" -->
## Getting your entity structure right from the start saves money later

The entity you choose shapes how much tax you pay, how you get paid, and how exposed your personal assets are if something goes wrong. A sole proprietorship is simple to start, but it offers no liability protection. An S-Corp can save real money on self-employment tax once your profit climbs, but it comes with payroll requirements and stricter rules. An LLC sits somewhere in between with flexibility on how it is taxed.

Pick the wrong structure and you can overpay for years or scramble to restructure mid-stream, which is rarely cheap or fun. Our Entity Type Analysis service looks at your expected income, your growth plans, and your appetite for paperwork, then recommends the structure that fits. We also handle the state filings so the entity you chose is the entity that actually exists on paper. Getting this decision right early is one of the quietest, most valuable things a new owner can do.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: secure-online-accounting-platform.jpg | alt: "Business owner reviewing financial dashboard on a laptop in a workshop" | query: "small business owner reviewing financials laptop" -->
## Your books, payroll, and taxes handled so you can run your business

You did not start a business to spend Sunday nights reconciling accounts. Our team handles the recurring work that keeps a company compliant and clear: bookkeeping with proper transaction classification, full-service payroll, and year-round tax planning so April is never a surprise.

Everything runs through a secure online platform that gives you real-time visibility into your numbers. Want to know your cash position before you sign a lease or hire your first employee? Log in and look. You get the clarity of a finance department without having to become an accountant yourself, and without chasing a spreadsheet you updated three weeks ago. When a tax question pops up in July, you reach a real person who already knows your business, not a seasonal hotline. That steadiness is the whole point: fewer fire drills, more time on the work that pays the bills.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: cfo-guidance-meeting.jpg | alt: "Advisor reviewing business performance charts with a startup owner" | query: "financial advisor meeting startup owner" -->
## CFO-level guidance without the CFO-level price tag

A full-time chief financial officer in Texas can cost well into six figures, which is out of reach for most new businesses. Our Business Management Services give you that strategic oversight at a fraction of the cost, bundled into the same fixed monthly fee. Think of it as the coaching layer that helps you make smarter calls as you grow.

- Regular financial reviews that translate your numbers into plain decisions
- Cash flow planning so you can see what is coming before it arrives
- Guidance on pricing, hiring, and major purchases backed by real data
- A retirement plan analysis when you are ready to set one up for yourself or your team
- A direct line to advisors who have helped Southeast Texas owners since 1950

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: established-port-arthur-cpa-firm.jpg | alt: "Established accounting firm team standing outside a Port Arthur office" | query: "professional accounting firm team office" -->
## Southeast Texas business owners have trusted us since 1950

We are not a pop-up app that launched last quarter. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has kept books, run payroll, and prepared taxes for Port Arthur businesses for more than seven decades. In that time we have served physicians, restaurants, dentists, agriculture operations, and plenty of brand-new ventures still figuring out their first hire.

That history matters most to first-time owners, because you are trusting someone with the financial backbone of the thing you just built. You are working with a stable, full-service firm that will still be here next tax season and the one after that. Small enough to know your name, established enough to have seen your situation before.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: start-business-consultation.jpg | alt: "New business owner shaking hands with an accountant across a desk" | query: "business owner consultation handshake office" -->
## Ready to start your business on the right foot

Think you are too small to need a CPA firm? That is exactly when the right setup saves you the most. The decisions you make in your first months follow you for years, and a short conversation now can keep you from expensive corrections later. There is no minimum size and no obligation, just a straightforward next step.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

## Resources

### Resources library | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/resources-library

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Plain-language guides to help you run a smarter business

This library was built for business owners, not accountants. The guides, magazines, and news briefs here answer the money questions you actually run into: how to read a financial statement, when to rethink your entity type, what a new tax rule means for your payroll. Whether you run a clinic in Port Arthur, a restaurant down the road, a dental practice, a farm, or a startup anywhere in Southeast Texas, the goal is the same. Read something here, walk away knowing your next move, and skip the jargon. No login required to browse.

<!-- block: content-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## eBooks worth bookmarking

These downloadable guides go deeper than a blog post and read faster than a textbook. Save one now, reach for it when the question comes up.

### How to read a financial statement
photo: reading-financial-statement.jpg
query: business owner reviewing financial statement

Balance sheets and P&Ls stop being a mystery once you know what each line is telling you. A practical walk-through for owners who sign the checks but never studied accounting.
[Read more](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/e-books/how-to-read-a-financial-statement)

### Maximizing your tax refund
photo: tax-planning-guide.jpg
query: small business owner tax planning desk

A strategic guide to planning ahead so April stops surprising you. Year-round moves that lower what you owe instead of last-minute scrambling.
[Read more](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/e-books/maximizing-your-tax-refund-a-strategic-guide-to-tax-planning)

### Everything to know about 1099s
photo: contractor-paperwork-1099.jpg
query: contractor filling out tax forms

Who gets one, when it is due, and how to avoid penalties. A clear reference for any business that pays contractors.
[Read more](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/e-books/everything-you-need-to-know-about-1099s)

### From success to succession planning
photo: succession-planning-meeting.jpg
query: business owner planning future office

The business you built deserves a handoff plan. Start thinking through ownership transfer long before you need to act.
[Read more](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/e-books/from-success-to-succession-planning)

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: advantage-magazine-reading.jpg | alt: "Business owner reading a financial magazine at an office desk" | query: "professional reading magazine office desk" -->
## Advantage Magazine, your quarterly financial read

Four times a year, Advantage Magazine lands with a mix of tax updates, small business strategy, and money topics you can act on. One issue might cover retirement plan choices for a growing practice. The next might break down cash-flow habits that keep restaurants and farms steady through a slow season. It is written for owners, not specialists, so the takeaways are concrete.

The magazine is also proof of something we believe: tax season is one month, but your financial questions run year-round. Advantage exists to keep useful information in front of you in March, July, and every month between. Browse the current issue and the archive in the [resources library](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/resources-library).

<!-- block: content-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## QuickReads, short takes on news that affects your bottom line

QuickReads are exactly that: brief, curated rundowns of tax law changes, economic shifts, and financial news picked because they matter to small business owners in Southeast Texas. No 2,000-word think pieces, no filler. Read one in two minutes, know whether it affects you, move on.

### Why small businesses go bankrupt and how to avoid it
photo: small-business-cash-flow.jpg
query: stressed small business owner finances

A finance expert names five cash-flow traps that sink small businesses, and the habits that keep yours out of them.
[Read more](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/quick-reads/why-small-businesses-are-going-bankrupt-and-how-to-avoid-it)

### Five things to do before a recession
photo: recession-planning-business.jpg
query: business owner reviewing budget planning

Downturns hit small businesses hardest because the runway is shortest. Five practical steps to take now, before the odds turn.
[Read more](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/quick-reads/with-odds-of-a-recession-going-up-here-are-5-things-small-businesses-should-do-now)

### Summer camps and the dependent care credit
photo: family-tax-credit-summer.jpg
query: parent reviewing childcare expenses paperwork

The dependent care tax credit reaches further than traditional day care. Here is how summer camp costs may qualify.
[Read more](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/quick-reads/summer-camps-may-qualify-for-dependent-care-tax-credit)

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: filter-resources-laptop.jpg | alt: "Business owner filtering financial articles on a laptop screen" | query: "person filtering content laptop screen" -->
## Filter resources by topic or business type

Nobody wants to scroll past 40 articles to find the one that fits. Narrow the library two ways and get to what is relevant faster.

- Filter by content type: eBooks, Advantage Magazine, or QuickReads
- Filter by business type to surface material written for your work:
- Physicians and medical practices
- Restaurants
- Dentists and dental practices
- Agriculture businesses
- New business owners launching their first venture

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Plain-language guides built for Southeast Texas business owners

Accounting topics get a bad rap for being dense and intimidating. They don't have to be. Since 1950, Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants has helped small businesses, nonprofits, and individuals across Southeast Texas make sense of their numbers, and these free eBooks carry that same plain-language approach.

Each guide pulls from work we do every day for physicians, restaurants, dentists, farms, and new business owners in and around Port Arthur. No jargon for the sake of jargon. No filler. Just clear, practical reading you can use before your next tax deadline, payroll run, or growth decision. Download what's useful, and reach out when you'd rather hand the work to a CPA team.

### Forms, documents and links | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/forms-documents-and-links

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Everything you need, organized in one place

Forms scattered across a dozen government websites waste your morning. This page pulls the most-requested items into one spot: client forms, IRS and Texas tax links, and the tools to send us your documents. Find what you need, hand off the rest, and get back to running your practice, restaurant, or farm. If something isn't here, the Port Arthur team is a phone call away.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: tax-forms-desk.jpg | alt: "Tax forms and financial documents organized on an office desk" | query: "tax forms paperwork desk" -->
## Tax and financial forms for individuals and businesses

Knowing which form applies is half the battle. Here are the ones clients in Southeast Texas reach for most, labeled in plain English so you don't second-guess yourself.

- **Form W-4**, Tell your employer how much federal tax to withhold from each paycheck.
- **Form W-9**, Share your taxpayer ID with a business that needs to issue you a 1099.
- **Form 1040**, The individual federal income tax return for personal filers.
- **Form 1099-NEC**, Report payments of $600 or more made to contractors and freelancers.
- **Form SS-4**, Apply for an Employer Identification Number when you start a business.
- **Texas Sales and Use Tax Return**, File and remit sales tax collected from your customers.
- **Form 941**, Report quarterly payroll taxes withheld from employee wages.

Not sure whether you need a W-9 or a 1099? That's exactly the kind of question our [tax](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax) and [payroll](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll) teams answer year-round.

<!-- block: feature-grid | variant: 3-col -->
## Government and agency links for common tasks

We've grouped the official links around what you actually came here to do, so you spend less time digging through menus.

### Check your federal refund
icon: DollarSign

Use the IRS "Where's My Refund?" tool to track your federal return status. Prefer to watch it with us? Try our [refund tracker](https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/refund-tracker).

### Make a federal tax payment
icon: CreditCard

IRS Direct Pay lets you send a payment straight from your bank account for individual taxes, estimated payments, and balances due.

### Handle Texas sales tax
icon: Receipt

The Texas Comptroller's Webfile system is where you register, file, and pay state sales and use tax for your business.

### Register a Texas business
icon: Building

The Texas Secretary of State handles entity formation and filings. Pair it with our [entity type analysis](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis) before you choose a structure.

### Manage Social Security
icon: UserCheck

The Social Security Administration site covers benefit estimates, replacement cards, and wage reporting for you and your employees.

### Look up an EIN
icon: FileText

Apply for or confirm your Employer Identification Number directly through the IRS online application.

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: vertical -->
## How to send documents to our team securely

Email is fine for lunch plans, not for your Social Security number. Our secure online platform keeps sensitive financial information protected, and it's open whenever you are, including nights and weekends.

### Log in to your secure portal
Use the credentials we set up during onboarding to reach your private client space from any device.

### Upload your files
Drag and drop tax documents, receipts, or statements. Everything is encrypted in transit and at rest.

### We get notified instantly
Your BBL accountant sees the upload right away and can start work without waiting on the mail.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: cpa-client-meeting.jpg | alt: "CPA reviewing documents with a small business owner in a Port Arthur office" | query: "accountant client meeting office" -->
## Not sure which form or document you need

Guessing at tax forms is how small mistakes turn into IRS letters. If you're staring at this page unsure where to start, just ask. Our Port Arthur team has guided Southeast Texas businesses and individuals since 1950, and we're available year-round, not only from January to April. Tell us what you're trying to accomplish and we'll point you to the right form, link, or service.
