### Refund tracker | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/resources/refund-tracker

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: refund-tracker-checklist.jpg | alt: "Person checking IRS refund status on a laptop next to tax documents" | query: "person checking tax refund status laptop" -->
## Where is my refund right now

Most refund questions have the same answer: check the source first. The IRS updates its [Where's My Refund](https://www.irs.gov/refunds) tool once every 24 hours, and it's faster than waiting on hold. Before you click through, have three things on hand.

- Your Social Security number or ITIN
- Your filing status (single, married filing jointly, head of household, etc.)
- The exact refund amount shown on your return, down to the cent

Texas doesn't collect a personal income tax, so there's no state income refund to chase down. If your business is waiting on a sales tax refund, that runs through the [Texas Comptroller's office](https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/sales-use/refunds.php) instead, on a separate timeline.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: refund-timeline-review.jpg | alt: "Accountant reviewing a tax return timeline on a desktop calendar" | query: "accountant reviewing tax timeline calendar" -->
## How long your refund typically takes to arrive

E-filed returns with direct deposit move fastest, usually landing within 21 days of IRS acceptance. Paper returns take longer, often 6 to 8 weeks, because someone has to enter them into the system by hand before processing even starts. Choosing a paper check instead of direct deposit tacks on another week or two at the end.

A few things slow the clock: math errors, a name that doesn't match Social Security records, claims for the Earned Income Tax Credit or Additional Child Tax Credit (these get extra scrutiny by law), or a return flagged for identity verification. None of that means something went wrong on your end. It usually means the IRS is doing exactly what it's supposed to do.

If your refund is running past these windows and you're a BBL client, call us. We prepared your return, so we can tell you in plain terms whether the wait is normal or worth a closer look.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: irs-notice-review.jpg | alt: "CPA reviewing an IRS notice letter with a client at a desk" | query: "accountant reviewing IRS notice letter" -->
## When to call the IRS versus when to call us

A refund running a few weeks behind schedule usually isn't an emergency. The IRS phone lines stay busy for a reason: most calls are about delays that resolve on their own. If your return shows "still processing" and it's been under 21 days for e-file or 8 weeks for paper, give it more time before you dial.

Call us instead when a notice shows up in the mail, your refund gets offset against a debt you didn't know about, you're asked to verify your identity in person, or a letter mentions an audit. Those moments call for someone who can read the notice, talk to the IRS directly, and respond before a deadline passes.

Our [Audit Protection](/what-we-do/audit-protection) service covers exactly this: representation on notices, letters, and proceedings, handled by people who already know your return. You won't be left reading IRS jargon alone at the kitchen table.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: year-round-tax-planning.jpg | alt: "Small business owner and accountant discussing tax planning documents" | query: "business owner accountant tax planning meeting" -->
## How BBL clients get more from their tax return every year

A bigger refund feels good, but it usually means you overpaid the IRS all year and handed them an interest-free loan. The better goal is keeping that money working in your business or your household, month by month, instead of waiting on it every April.

That's the point of year-round tax planning. We look at your numbers throughout the year, not just at filing time, so withholding, estimated payments, and deductions get adjusted before they cost you anything. [Physicians](/industries/physicians), [dentists](/industries/dentists), restaurant owners, and agriculture operations across Southeast Texas each run on different rhythms, and a once-a-year conversation misses most of the opportunity.

Our [Tax](/what-we-do/tax) service includes strategic planning and preparation through one secure digital platform, so you're never digging through old emails to find last year's numbers. For a deeper look at the planning side, our [tax refund strategy guide](/resources/e-books/maximizing-your-tax-refund-a-strategic-guide-to-tax-planning) walks through it step by step.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: cpa-consultation-handshake.jpg | alt: "CPA shaking hands with a small business client after a consultation" | query: "accountant client handshake office meeting" -->
## Ready to stop guessing and start planning

You don't have to guess at your refund, or your tax bill, every year. Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore has guided Southeast Texas business owners and individuals through tax season since 1950, with a fixed monthly fee that means no billing surprises and a secure platform that shows you where things stand all year. [Schedule a consultation](/contact) and let's figure out what your numbers should be doing for you.

---

### Quick reads for small business owners
URL: /resources/quick-reads

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Small business accounting, minus the headache

Running a business in Port Arthur means wearing every hat at once, often before lunch. These quick reads cut accounting down to size: short, practical tips you can act on between customers, patients, or planting seasons. No theory. No jargon. Just the habits and decisions that keep more money in your pocket and the IRS out of your inbox.

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: clean-books-bookkeeping.jpg | alt: "Small business owner organizing receipts and reviewing books at a desk" | query: "small business owner organizing receipts" -->
## Quick wins that keep your books clean year-round

Clean books are not an accident, and they are not a once-a-year scramble at tax time. They come from a few small routines you repeat until they feel automatic. None of this requires an accounting degree. It requires showing up consistently.

Start with these three habits this week:

- **Categorize transactions as they happen.** Tagging a purchase the day it posts takes seconds. Sorting nine months of mystery charges in March takes hours and usually costs you deductions you can no longer prove.
- **Reconcile on a set cadence.** Match your books to your bank and card statements every month, not every quarter. Monthly reconciliation catches duplicate charges, missed deposits, and fraud while you can still do something about it.
- **Keep personal and business money apart.** One business checking account and one business card. When you mix the two, every report gets muddy and an audit gets harder to defend.

If your current setup already feels behind, our [bookkeeping team](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting) can clean it up and hand you a system you can actually maintain.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: payroll-management-tips.jpg | alt: "Business owner processing employee payroll on a laptop in an office" | query: "business owner processing payroll laptop" -->
## How to stay ahead of payroll without losing your mind

Payroll punishes lateness. Miss a federal tax deposit deadline and the penalty starts at a percentage of what you owe, climbing the longer it sits. Knowing your deposit schedule, monthly or semi-weekly, is the first thing to nail down.

The second is classification. Calling a worker a contractor when the IRS sees an employee is one of the most expensive mistakes a Port Arthur small business can make, because back taxes and penalties land on you, not the worker. When in doubt, look at how much control you have over the work and the hours.

The third is your platform. A secure online payroll system calculates withholdings, files the right forms, and timestamps every deposit so you are not relying on memory or a sticky note. Fewer manual steps means fewer costly errors. Our [payroll service](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll) runs all of this through one secure platform, so you can see exactly where things stand without chasing paperwork.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: tax-deductions-southeast-texas.jpg | alt: "Accountant reviewing tax deduction documents with a restaurant owner" | query: "accountant reviewing tax documents client" -->
## Tax deductions Southeast Texas business owners often miss

Most overlooked deductions are not exotic. They are ordinary expenses that owners forget to track or assume do not qualify.

Restaurant owners routinely miss the deductible portion of business meals, spoilage and waste write-offs, and the cost of uniforms and equipment repairs. Physicians and dentists often overlook continuing education, professional licensing fees, malpractice premiums, and the depreciation on expensive imaging or operatory equipment. Agriculture operations leave money on the table when they fail to track fuel use, equipment depreciation, and certain land improvement costs.

Across every small business, the usual suspects slip through: home office expenses, mileage logged on a personal vehicle, software subscriptions, and a slice of your cell phone bill.

Here is the catch. You can only claim what you can document, and documentation happens during the year, not after it ends. That is the whole point of year-round [tax planning](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax) instead of a single April conversation. We watch the deductions as they accumulate so nothing gets stranded.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: business-entity-selection.jpg | alt: "New business owner reviewing entity structure options with an advisor" | query: "new business owner advisor meeting" -->
## Choosing the right business entity saves you money from day one

The structure you picked when you launched may be quietly costing you. A sole proprietorship is simple, but it offers no liability protection and exposes all your profit to self-employment tax. An LLC adds a legal shield between your business and your personal assets. An S-corp can lower your self-employment tax bill once your profit crosses a certain threshold, because you split income between salary and distributions.

The right answer depends on your profit, your growth plans, and how much risk your work carries. A new dental practice and a seasonal agriculture operation should not default to the same structure.

Most Texas owners never revisit this decision after launch, even as their revenue doubles. That is a missed opportunity worth real dollars every year. Our [Entity Type Analysis](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/entity-type-analysis) walks you through the trade-offs in plain language and runs the numbers on what each option would actually cost you.

<!-- block: content-prose | variant: standard -->
## Why your accounting system is either working for you or against you

There is no neutral setting. A messy or outdated accounting system quietly bleeds money: missed deductions because expenses were never categorized, payroll errors because the data was wrong going in, and audit risk because the numbers do not reconcile. You feel it as vague stress and surprise tax bills.

A system built correctly does the opposite. Reports come out clean. Tax prep takes days, not weeks. You can answer a lender or the IRS without a panic.

The fix is usually a one-time effort with years of payoff. Our [Accounting System Setup](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting-system-setup) gets the foundation right, whether you are starting fresh or untangling something that grew out of control.

<!-- block: stats-bar | variant: 3-up -->
## Seven decades of guiding Southeast Texas businesses

### 1950
Founded in Port Arthur, Texas

### 70+
Years handling tax, payroll, and audits for Southeast Texas businesses

### $0
Surprise bills, thanks to fixed monthly pricing

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: hand-off-accounting-pro.jpg | alt: "Business owner shaking hands with a CPA across an office desk" | query: "business owner handshake accountant office" -->
## When to stop doing it yourself and hand it to a pro

DIY accounting works right up until it doesn't. A few signs you have outgrown it: your revenue has grown past the point where spreadsheets keep up, you have added staff and payroll now eats your evenings, you have received an IRS notice you are not sure how to answer, or you simply have no hours left to run the business you actually started.

If any of those sound familiar, you are not behind. You are at the normal point where the math favors handing it off. With a fixed monthly fee, a secure online platform for real-time visibility, year-round tax support, and CFO-level guidance at a fraction of the cost, you trade guesswork for clarity. Take a look at our [Business Management Services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services) to see what that support looks like.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: schedule-consultation-cpa.jpg | alt: "CPA team welcoming a small business owner for a consultation" | query: "professional consultation meeting office" -->
## Let us guide you toward a healthy financial future

Bring your questions, your shoebox of receipts, or just your gut feeling that something could run smoother. We have helped Southeast Texas business owners sort it out since 1950, and the first conversation costs you nothing but a little time.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

## Other

### Home | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: port-arthur-office-history.jpg | alt: "Exterior of a CPA firm office building in downtown Port Arthur Texas" | query: "small town accounting office exterior" -->
## Serving Southeast Texas businesses since 1950

Port Arthur looked different in 1950, when Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore first opened its doors. The refineries were younger, Jefferson County was smaller, and a handful of local business owners needed someone they could trust with the numbers. Seventy-five years later, that's still the job. The firm has guided small business owners, physicians, dentists, restaurant operators, and farmers across Southeast Texas through oil busts, hurricanes, and a tax code that never stops changing.

Longevity like that doesn't happen by accident. It comes from answering the phone, showing up for the audit, and getting the return filed correctly, year after year. Meet the people behind that track record on our [team page](/our-team), or read more about [who we are](/who-we-are).

<!-- block: checklist-section | variant: with-image | image: fixed-fee-accounting-dashboard.jpg | alt: "Accountant reviewing a monthly fee summary with a business client" | query: "accountant client reviewing invoice paperwork" -->
## One predictable monthly fee covers everything you need

Surprise invoices are stressful, and in accounting they're common. At Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore, outsourced accounting clients pay one fixed monthly fee. No add-on charge when payroll runs an extra cycle. No surprise bill because a tax question took an extra phone call. The scope is set up front, so you know exactly what leaves your account every month, the same way you budget for rent or insurance.

That fee bundles real work, not vague promises. It covers bookkeeping and transaction classification, payroll processing, strategic tax planning and preparation, and access to a secure online platform where you can check your numbers anytime, not just at tax time. Audit protection and year-round support are built into the package too, so a letter from the IRS doesn't turn into a separate bill on top of everything else.

Explore the full [outsourced accounting](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting) service to see what's included for your business.

<!-- block: industry-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## We work with the businesses that keep Southeast Texas running

Southeast Texas runs on a specific mix of businesses: medical and dental practices, restaurants, farms and ranches, and small business owners who wear every hat in the company. Each one has different tax rules, different cash flow patterns, and different blind spots. Industry-specific guidance catches what generic bookkeeping tends to miss.

### Physicians
icon: Stethoscope

Practice owners juggle payer reimbursements, overhead, and staffing costs. We help [physicians](/industries/physicians) manage the financial side so patient care stays the focus.

### Dentists
icon: HeartPulse

Equipment financing, associate buy-ins, and practice transitions all carry tax consequences. See how we support [dentists](/industries/dentists) through every stage of ownership.

### Restaurants
icon: Receipt

Thin margins and high turnover demand tight cost controls. Our [restaurant](/industries/restaurants) clients get payroll, cost-aware bookkeeping, and tax planning built for the business.

### Agriculture
icon: Globe

Crop cycles, equipment depreciation, and land transitions need accounting that understands the calendar. Southeast Texas [agriculture](/industries/agriculture) operations rely on us for exactly that.

### Small business owners
icon: Briefcase

From a single location to a growing team, [small business owners](/industries/small-business-owners) need numbers they can act on, not just file away.

### New business owners
icon: Lightbulb

Entity selection, accounting setup, and startup tax decisions made early save money for years. [New business owners](/industries/new-business-owners) get that foundation built right from day one.

Browse all [industries](/industries) we serve across Southeast Texas.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-left | image: secure-online-platform-laptop.jpg | alt: "Business owner viewing a financial dashboard on a laptop screen" | query: "business owner laptop financial dashboard" -->
## Real-time financial visibility without the guesswork

Most business owners find out how their company did last quarter three weeks after it mattered. A secure online platform changes that. Log in any time, day or night, and see bookkeeping, payroll, and financial statements as they're updated, not after a phone call and a week of waiting.

That kind of visibility matters most when you're deciding whether to hire, buy equipment, or take a draw. You shouldn't need an accounting degree to read your own numbers, and you shouldn't need an appointment to check them. The platform gives Southeast Texas business owners a current view of where the business stands, with the accounting team a message away when a number needs explaining.

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## From startup paperwork to CFO-level strategy, we handle it all

Running a business means more financial work than most owners signed up for. Here's what gets handled, from day-one paperwork to long-term strategy.

### Outsourced Accounting
icon: Calculator

A fully bundled, fixed-fee service covering [bookkeeping, payroll, and tax](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting) in one monthly plan, delivered through our secure platform.

### Accounting and Bookkeeping
icon: ClipboardCheck

Transaction classification, reconciliations, and financial statements handled by a seasoned team. See the full [accounting and bookkeeping](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting) scope.

### Payroll
icon: Users

Full-service [payroll](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll) management, including filings and compliance, run through a secure online system.

### Tax
icon: FileText

Strategic [business and personal tax](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax) planning and preparation, not just a once-a-year filing scramble.

### Audit Protection
icon: ShieldCheck

If the IRS sends a notice, we handle it. [Audit protection](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/audit-protection) covers representation at no extra charge.

### Business Management Services
icon: ChartLine

CFO-level oversight, budgeting, and reporting through [business management services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services), at a fraction of an in-house executive's salary.

### Business Foundation Services
icon: Building2

Entity selection, accounting setup, and startup advisory through [business foundation services](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-foundation-services) for businesses just getting off the ground.

See the full [services](/services) list for more detail.

You didn't start a medical practice, a restaurant, a farm, or a small business to spend your evenings reconciling accounts. Hand that part to a firm that's done it for Southeast Texas businesses since 1950, with one predictable fee and a secure platform that keeps you in the loop.

A consultation costs you nothing but a conversation. Tell us where your books stand today, and we'll show you what a fixed-fee plan looks like for your business.

### Get started with BBL
URL: /get-started

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Your first step toward stress-free accounting starts here

Getting started with BBL is simple, and there are no wrong answers here. Tell us where your business stands today, and we will take it from there. No spreadsheets to clean up first. No judgment about that shoebox of receipts. Since 1950, we have helped Southeast Texas physicians, restaurant owners, farmers, and small business owners hand off the accounting headaches and get back to running their businesses. The first conversation costs you nothing but a few minutes, and it sets the tone for everything that follows: clear, calm, and built around your needs.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: color-bg -->
## Tell us a little about your business

Think of this as a conversation starter, not an application. Tell us your name, what kind of business you run, and the one accounting problem keeping you up at night. Maybe payroll eats your Fridays. Maybe tax season sneaks up every year. Whatever it is, share it, and we will reach out to talk through how BBL can lighten the load. No commitment, no pressure, just a starting point.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

<!-- block: process-steps | variant: vertical -->
## Here is what happens after you reach out

### We start with a real conversation
Your first consultation is a chance for us to listen. You tell us about your business, your pain points, and what you want off your plate. We ask questions. Nobody pulls out a contract.

### We review what you actually need
Next, we look at where your books stand and what your business requires, from monthly bookkeeping to year-round tax planning. If your accounting system needs a cleanup before we begin, we will say so plainly.

### We match you to the right bundle
Based on that review, we recommend the specific services that fit your business, whether that is payroll alone or full outsourced accounting with CFO-level oversight. You only pay for what you use.

### We hand you a fixed-fee proposal
Here is the part business owners love: one flat monthly fee, written out in advance. You see exactly what is included before you say yes. No hourly surprises, no end-of-month invoices that make you wince.

### We set up your secure platform
Once you are on board, we get you onto our secure online platform so you can see your financials in real time, from anywhere. From there, the busywork becomes ours, and your numbers become clear.

<!-- block: service-cards | variant: 3-col -->
## Services we can bundle for you from day one

### Outsourced Accounting
icon: Calculator

A fully bundled accounting service for one fixed monthly fee, delivered through our secure online platform. This is the everything-handled option for owners who want to stop thinking about the books entirely. [Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting)

### Bookkeeping
icon: FileText

Comprehensive bookkeeping from a seasoned team, including transaction classification and clean, accurate records you can actually read. The foundation everything else rests on. [Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/accounting)

### Payroll
icon: Users

Full-service payroll run through a secure online platform, covering your team, your filings, and your deadlines. Get your Fridays back. [Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/payroll)

### Tax
icon: Receipt

Strategic business tax planning and preparation handled digitally, with support that lasts all year instead of one frantic week in April. [Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/tax)

### Audit Protection
icon: ShieldCheck

If the IRS sends a notice, letter, or proceeding, we handle it. Expert representation so you never face the agency alone. [Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/audit-protection)

### Business Management Services
icon: ChartLine

CFO-level financial oversight at a fraction of the cost of a full-time hire, including regular financial reviews and guidance built for decision-making. [Learn more](https://www.bblcpa.com/what-we-do/business-management-services)

### Contact | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/contact

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## We're ready when you are

Reaching out shouldn't feel like a sales pitch, and at Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore it doesn't. Whether you call, email, or fill out the form below, you'll hear back from a real person on our Port Arthur team, not an automated queue. Think of this as step one toward clearer books and fewer financial guessing games, not a commitment to anything. Our office hours and address are listed below so you can reach us however works best for you.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## How to reach our Port Arthur accounting team

Call, email, or stop by, whichever fits your schedule. Every contact method connects you straight to our Port Arthur office, the same team that's handled tax returns, payroll runs, and audits for Southeast Texas businesses since 1950. No call center, no script reading, no being passed between departments.

When you dial our number or send a message, you're reaching the people who will actually do the work, the same accountants who know the difference between a restaurant's seasonal cash flow and a dental practice's equipment financing needs. Office hours, phone, email, and our address are listed just below this section, ready whenever you are.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: color-bg -->
## Tell us a little about your situation

You don't need a perfectly organized shoebox of receipts or a ten-point agenda to reach out. A sentence or two about what's keeping you up at night, messy books, payroll headaches, a tax notice you don't understand, is plenty. Our team reviews every submission personally, usually within one business day, through the same secure online platform we use for client accounting. No interrogation, no judgment, just a starting point.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: small-business-owner-meeting.jpg | alt: "Small business owner discussing finances with an accountant at a desk" | query: "small business owner meeting accountant" -->
## Not sure where to start, we can help with that too

Plenty of business owners who call us aren't sure if they need a bookkeeper, a CPA, or just someone to make sense of last year's tax mess. That's normal, and it's exactly the conversation we're built for. New business owners ask about entity structure and [business foundation services](/what-we-do/business-foundation-services) all the time. Restaurant owners often start with payroll headaches and end up moving to fully [outsourced accounting](/what-we-do/outsourced-accounting). Physicians and dentists frequently come in wanting [tax](/what-we-do/tax) planning and discover they need CFO-level oversight more than they realized. We'll ask a few questions about your business, your industry, and what's actually costing you time or money, then point you toward the right fit. You don't have to diagnose the problem before you call.

<!-- block: form | variant: contact -->
## What happens after you reach out

Submit the form below or call our office, and here's what happens next. You'll hear from a member of our team within one business day, usually sooner. We'll set up a short call, fifteen to twenty minutes, to understand your business, your industry, and what's eating up your time. We'll walk you through fixed monthly fee options for accounting, payroll, or tax support, with no surprise invoices and no pressure to sign anything on the spot.

If you've got a tax notice sitting on your desk or need IRS representation, tell us up front. We move faster on those. Once you're a client, everything runs through our secure online platform, so you get real-time visibility into your numbers from day one.

### Privacy policy | Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants
URL: https://www.bblcpa.com/privacy-policy

# Privacy policy

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Introduction and scope of this policy

This policy covers how Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants collects, uses, and protects information from anyone who visits bblcpa.com, calls our Port Arthur office, or works with us as a client. We know you're trusting us with sensitive financial details, tax records, and payroll data, so we've written this in plain English instead of dense legal boilerplate. It applies to our website, our secure online platform, and the intake process for every service we offer, from bookkeeping to audit protection. If something here doesn't make sense, call us. That's what we're here for.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Information we collect when you use our site or services

When you browse bblcpa.com, we collect standard analytics data: pages visited, browser type, and approximate location, gathered through cookies and similar tools. If you submit a contact form or request a consultation, we collect your name, phone number, email, and any business details you choose to share.

Once you become a client, the scope expands to match the service. Outsourced accounting and bookkeeping engagements involve bank and credit card transactions. Payroll services require employee names, wages, and tax identification numbers. Tax preparation, whether business or personal, involves income records, prior returns, and supporting documentation. All of this active client data is collected through secure intake forms or uploaded directly to our online platform, never through unencrypted email when we can help it.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## How we use your information

We use your information for exactly the work you've hired us to do, nothing more. Payroll data runs payroll and files the related tax forms. Bookkeeping records feed into the financial statements and dashboards available on our secure online platform, giving you real-time visibility into your numbers. Tax information supports preparation, filing, and the year-round planning that's part of our service, not a once-a-year scramble.

For business management clients, we also use financial data to build the budgets, forecasts, and KPI reports that come with CFO-level guidance. Contact form submissions are used only to respond to your inquiry and, if you agree, to send occasional updates relevant to your business.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## How we protect and secure your data

Financial and payroll data deserves more than a password and good intentions. Our secure online platform uses encryption for data in transit and at rest, and access is restricted to the team members actually working on your account. We don't email sensitive documents as unprotected attachments, and we don't store client records on personal devices.

Internally, staff access is role-based, meaning a bookkeeper doesn't see payroll files for clients outside their assignment, and a payroll specialist doesn't have open access to tax planning notes. We review our platform's security settings regularly and update credentials whenever staffing changes. If you ever want a plain-language explanation of how a specific document is stored, ask your engagement contact directly.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Third-party sharing and disclosure

We share client information only where the service requires it. Tax filings go to the IRS and Texas state agencies. Payroll data goes to the payroll processor and relevant tax authorities for deposits and filings. If you're under IRS examination and we're handling your audit protection, we communicate with the IRS on your behalf, with your authorization on file.

We do not sell client data. We do not share your financial records with marketers, data brokers, or unrelated third parties, and we never will. Disclosure outside normal service delivery happens only if required by law or court order.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Your rights and how to contact us about this policy

You can ask us at any time what information we hold about you, request a correction, or ask us to delete records we're not legally required to retain (tax documents, for example, often carry mandatory retention periods). You can also opt out of non-essential marketing emails without affecting your service relationship with us.

To make any of these requests, or if you have questions about this policy, reach our Port Arthur team directly. We've answered privacy questions from physicians, restaurant owners, and small business clients across Southeast Texas for decades, and we're glad to walk through ours with you too.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: color-bg -->
## Ready to talk about your finances instead of fine print

If you've read this far, you clearly care about how your information is handled, which is exactly the kind of client we like working with. Schedule a consultation and let's talk about what fixed-fee, real-time, year-round support actually looks like for your business.

[Schedule a consultation](https://www.bblcpa.com/contact)

---

### Terms of use
URL: /terms-of-use

<!-- block: intro-text | variant: centered -->
## Agreement to these terms

Welcome to the website of Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore CPAs & Consultants. When you visit this site or use any of the tools and services connected to it, you agree to the terms laid out on this page. Think of it as the ground rules that keep things clear and fair for everyone. If any part of these terms doesn't sit right with you, please hold off on using the site. By browsing, downloading a resource, or logging into our secure platform, you accept everything described below.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## How you may use this website and its content

The articles, guides, e-books, and calculators on this site are here to help small business owners, physicians, dentists, restaurant operators, and individuals across Southeast Texas make smarter financial decisions. You're welcome to read, print, and share our content for your own personal or internal business use.

A few things to keep in mind. Please don't copy, republish, or sell our material as your own, scrape the site with automated tools, or use anything here in a way that breaks the law or interferes with how the site runs. The Brammer, Begnaud, & Lattimore name, logo, and original content belong to the firm and are protected accordingly.

We update content regularly, so figures and guidance can change without notice. If you plan to act on something you read, confirm it with our team first so you're working from current numbers.

<!-- block: content-split | variant: image-right | image: secure-financial-data.jpg | alt: "Person logging into a secure financial platform on a laptop" | query: "secure online banking laptop professional" -->
## Privacy, data security, and your information

Clients in healthcare, dentistry, and food service hand us some of their most sensitive numbers, and protecting that information is non-negotiable. When you use this site or our secure online platform, we collect only what's needed to deliver your accounting, payroll, and tax services and to keep your account working smoothly.

Your financial data moves through encrypted, password-protected channels, not email attachments or open inboxes. We never sell your information, and we share it only when you ask us to or when the law requires it. The platform gives you real-time visibility into your own books while keeping that access locked to you and your authorized team.

For the full picture of how we gather, store, and handle data, read our [privacy policy](https://www.bblcpa.com/privacy-policy). Have a specific concern? Bring it to us directly and we'll walk you through exactly how your records are protected.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Disclaimers on accounting and tax information

The general content on this website is meant to inform, not to serve as formal accounting, tax, or legal advice for your specific situation. Tax law shifts, deadlines move, and what's right for a Port Arthur restaurant may be wrong for an agriculture operation down the road. A blog post can't account for the details that make your case yours.

Before you file, restructure, or make a financial decision based on something you found here, talk to a CPA who can look at your full picture. That's what year-round tax support is for. Reading an article is a fine first step. Working with our team turns that information into a plan built around your actual numbers.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Links to third-party sites and tools

To make your life easier, this site links out to outside resources like payroll platforms, IRS pages, and refund trackers. Those links are a convenience, nothing more. Once you leave our site, you're on someone else's turf, and their terms and privacy practices apply, not ours.

We don't control third-party content and can't take responsibility for its accuracy, security, or availability. If a linked tool causes you trouble, that's between you and the provider. When in doubt about whether an outside service fits your needs, ask us before you sign up or share data.

<!-- block: content-prose -->
## Governing law and jurisdiction for these terms

These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Texas, without regard to conflict-of-law rules. Any dispute that arises from your use of this site or our services falls under the jurisdiction of the state and federal courts serving Southeast Texas, including those covering Jefferson County and the Port Arthur area.

We've called Port Arthur home since 1950, and that local footing shapes how we operate, including how these agreements are handled. By using the site, you agree to settle any matters in those Texas courts.

<!-- block: cta-banner | variant: image-bg | image: port-arthur-cpa-consultation.jpg | alt: "CPA shaking hands with a small business owner in an office" | query: "accountant client handshake consultation office" -->
## Questions about these terms or ready to get started

If anything on this page raises a question, the answer is one conversation away. Our Port Arthur office has guided small businesses, nonprofits, and individuals across Southeast Texas for more than 70 years, and we're glad to explain how our terms, services, and secure platform work before you commit to anything.

[Schedule a consultation](/contact)

---
